Now that we have learned about the emergence of Indo Europeans, today we are going to learn more about ancient Europe and how it was affected by the Indo-European migrations. On our timeline we will be discussing a period of time that stretches from 7000 to 1100 BC. So this is what that looks like on our big 12,000-year timeline. In previous classes, we've learned how farming spread into Europe from the Middle East beginning about 7000 BC. First it spread to the Mediterranean and Greece, and then it reached the Italian coast and
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史课程。 我们已经了解了印欧人的出现，今 天我们将学习更多关于古代欧洲 的知识，以及它是如何受到印欧移 民的影响的。 在时间线上，我们将讨论从公元前 7000-1100 年间的这段时间。这就 是它在我们 12,000 年时间线上的 样子。 在往期课程中，我们已经了解到， 从大约公元前 7000 年开始，农业 是如何从中东传播到欧洲的。首 先，它传播到地中海和希腊，然后 到达了意大利海岸和匈牙利平原。 这些初代农民可能通经由多瑙河

paragraphs.en: Hungarian plain. These first farmers probably brought agriculture into the interior of Europe by travelling up the Danube River. There was a delay of several centuries between 6000 and 5000 BC as these first farmers had to adapt their farming techniques to the European climate. But they had solved these problems by 5500 BC, and farming spread even faster. By 5000 BC it had spread all the way up the Danube River and had also reached the entire Mediterranean coast. Then from 5000 to 3000 BC it spread to the rest of Europe. The culture that brought farming into the Interior of Europe is known as the Linear Pottery Culture because of the style of decorations
paragraphs.zh: 把农业带进了欧洲内陆。 在公元前 6000-5000 年间，由于这 些初代农民不得不更新他们的农 业技术以适应欧洲气候，所以在公 元前 6000-5000 年间有了几个世 纪的延迟。但他们在公元前 5500 年就解决了这些问题，农业的传播 速度比以前更快了。到公元前5000 年，农业一直延伸到了多瑙河，也 到达了整个地中海沿岸。 然后在公元前 5000-3000 年，农业 传播到了欧洲的其他地方。 把农业带入欧洲内部的文化，因其 陶器的装饰风格而被称为“线纹陶 文化”。这种文化也因人们在长屋 中生活的方式而与众不同。

paragraphs.en: on their clay pots. This culture is also distinctive for the way its people lived communally in longhouses. The Linear Pottery Culture existed from roughly 5500 to 4500 BC. After 4500 BC, there are dozens of different European farming cultures that we could examine. But we don't have time to look at them all, so we are just going to focus on a few. From 4300 to 2800 BC, one of the most significant European farming cultures was the Funnelbeaker culture. This culture existed for about 1500 years, mainly in northern Europe in what is now Poland, Germany, Denmark and southern Sweden. Because none of these cultures had developed writing, we only have
paragraphs.zh: 线 纹 陶 文 化 存 在 于 约 公 元 前 在公元前 4500 年之后，有数十种 不同的欧洲农耕文化可供研究。但 是我们时间有限，所以我们只关注 其中几个。 从公元前 4300-2800 年，欧洲最重 要的农业文化之一就是漏斗杯文 化。这种文化存在了大约 1500 年， 主要分布于北欧，即现在的波兰， 德国，丹麦和瑞典南部。 由于这些文化都没有发明文字，我 们对它们的了解有限。他们留下的

paragraphs.en: limited knowledge about them. And the most durable things that they left behind were their pots and other ceramic objects. So archaeologists classify them according to their style of pottery. The Funnelbeaker culture is so named because many of its pots have this distinctive funnel shape as you can see in this picture. The Funnelbeaker culture is also significant, because it was during this period that the European tradition of the passage tombs and communal graves reached its greatest extent. This is an example of one of these Funnelbeaker passage tombs. This particular tomb is located on the island of Bogø in Denmark, and it is called the Hulehøj passage grave.
paragraphs.zh: 最耐用的东西是锅和其他陶器。因 此，考古学家根据他们的陶器风格 对他们进行分类。 漏斗杯文化之所以如此命名，是因 为它的许多罐子都有独特的漏斗 形状，就像你们在这幅图中看到的 那样。 漏斗杯文化也很重要，因为在这一 时期，欧洲的通道墓和公共坟墓的 传统达到顶峰。 这是漏斗杯文化的一个通道墓。这 个墓穴位于丹麦的博岛，被称为 “胡利耶尔通道墓”。

paragraphs.en: This is also the time-period when the Neolithic Europeans began building megalithic monuments all across the continent. For example, the Funnelbeaker culture built the Harhoog megalith around 3000 BC which a good example of this type of monument. "Mega" means "big" and "Lithic" means "stone", so megalithic means big stone. But the most famous of all megalithic stone circles is probably Stone-Henge, located in southern England. This monument was built over several centuries, but the earliest construction probably began in 3000 BC and was probably constructed by the people who were living in England at that time and who were
paragraphs.zh: 也是在这段时期，新石器时代的欧 洲人开始在整个欧洲大陆建造巨 石纪念碑。 例如，漏斗杯文化在公元前 3000 年左右建造了哈胡格巨石，这是这 类纪念碑的一个很好的例子。 “mega”的意思是“大”，而 “lithic”则表示“石头”，因此 “megalith”的意思是“巨石”。 最著名的巨石石圈就是位于英格 兰南部的巨石阵了。 这座纪念碑是在几个世纪的时间 里逐渐建成的，但是最早的建筑可 能是在公元前 3000 年开始的，建 造者是当时居住在英格兰、与漏斗 杯文化有关的人群。

paragraphs.en: related to the Funnelbeaker culture. At around the same time that Stonehenge was being built, up to the north in the Orkney archipelago of Scotland another culture lived in a village that consisted of eight houses built out of stone and buried in the earth. Today we call this site Skara Brae. The settlement probably never had more than 50 people living in it, but it is so well preserved that it has become an important type-site for this period in Neolithic history. The village was occupied from 3100 BC until about 2500 BC and the people probably survived through a combination of raising cattle and sheep, eating seaweed and catching fish, and growing barley. The people who lived in Skara Brae
paragraphs.zh: 大约在建造巨石阵的同时，在苏格 兰北部奥克尼群岛，创造了另一个 文化的人群生活在一个由八栋埋 在地里的石屋构成的村庄里。 现在我们将其称为斯卡拉布雷新 石器时代遗迹。这个定居点可能从 来没有超过 50 个人聚居，但是它 保存得很好，已经成为新石器时代 的一个标准遗址。 在公元前 3100-2500 年间一直有 人在这个村庄生活，人们可能通过 饲养牛羊、吃海草、捕鱼和种植大 麦为生。 考古学家把生活在斯卡拉布雷遗

paragraphs.en: are known as the Grooved Ware people to archaeologists. Finally, in Ireland, we see what I think is the most impressive passage tomb of all, the passage tomb at Newgrange just a few miles from the city of Drogheda. The monument makes use of dramatic shifts in color as it alternates between white stones and black stones. The large entrance stone is embellished with decorative carvings, and the entire site lines up with the position of the rising sun on the winter solstice. Today Newgrange is a UNESCO world heritage site. But the Megalithic construction did not just happen in northern Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 址的人们称为槽皿人。 最令人印象更深刻的通道墓：纽格 莱奇墓位于爱尔兰，距离德罗赫达 仅有几英里。 这座纪念碑利用了颜色的戏剧性 变化，交替使用白色与黑色石头。 入口巨石装饰有花纹雕饰，整个遗 址都按照冬至日太阳升起的位置 排列。 如今，纽格莱奇墓是联合国教科文 组织认证的世界遗产。 但这种巨石建筑并不仅仅出现在 欧洲北部。大约在公元前 3600 年，

paragraphs.en: On the Island of Malta, in the Mediterranean, several large stone temples were constructed beginning around 3600 BC. These temples may be the oldest free-standing structures on earth. All of this megalithic construction was taking place while another big change was also occurring. Copper smelting was discovered in both the middle east and in Southern Europe and began to slowly spread across the continent, moving like agriculture, from south to north, and from east to west. Around 3300 BC, a man living in the Italian alps was killed in a fight with another person and then frozen by a mountain glacier. He remained frozen in that glacier for over 5000 years, preserved by
paragraphs.zh: 地中海的马耳他岛也开始建造了 几座大型的石庙。 这些神庙可能是地球上最古老的 独立建筑。 巨石阵在建造的同时，另一项重大 改变也正在发生。在中东和南欧都 发现了铜的冶炼，并开始缓慢地对 外传播——与农业一样，从南到 北，从东到西。 大约在公元前 3300 年，一个住在 意大利阿尔卑斯山的人在与另一 人的战斗中被杀，然后被一座高山 冰川冻住了。 他被冻在那座冰山中超过 5000 年，尸体被保留了下来。

paragraphs.en: the ice. And then, in 1991 two hikers discovered his body.At first they thought it was the corpse of someone who had died recently. But after scientists examined his body, they realized that he was thousands of years old. The press nicknamed him Otzi, and he is the oldest natural human mummy in the world. On the left is a picture of the mummy today, and on the right is a reconstruction of what he might have looked like while he was alive. Otzi has provided us with excellent information about life in Chalcolithic Europe. He carried a copper axe, a flint knife, a bow and arrows, boots, clothes made of woven grass and sewn skins.
paragraphs.zh: 1991 年，两个徒步旅行者发现了 他的尸体。一开始，他们以为这是 最近死去的人的尸体。但是在科学 家检查了他的身体之后，他们意识 到这具尸体已经有几千年的历史 了。 媒体称他为冰人奥兹，他是世界上 最古老的自然木乃伊。 左边是这具木乃伊的照片，右边是 他活着时可能的样子。 奥兹为我们提供了关于铜石并用 时期欧洲人生活的绝好信息。他手 持一把铜斧、一把火石刀、一把弓 和箭，穿着靴子、用编织的草做成 的衣服，还有缝制的皮。

paragraphs.en: He also had tatoos, and was in his mid-40s when he died. He also had arthritis and and seems to have had cavities and cardiovascular disease. Then around 2900 BC, a new people began to arrive in Eastern and Northern Europe. These were the Indo-Europeans, or Yamna people, who we learned about last class. They are called the "Corded Ware" people because their pottery has a decoration style that is made by pressing cords of twisted fiber into the clay before baking it. The Corded Ware people were probably a mixture of the new Indo-Europeans and the native Europeans. But we think the Indo-Europeans became the
paragraphs.zh: 他身上还有纹身，去世时大约四十 多岁。 他还患有关节炎，而且还可能有蛀 牙和心血管疾病。 大约在公元前 2900 年，又有一群 人进入了东欧和北欧。他们就是上 节课我们提到的印欧人和亚穆纳 人。 他们的文化被称为“绳纹器文化”， 因为他们喜爱在陶土上用绳索压 出花纹后，再用火烘烤，形成其独 特的陶器风格。 绳纹器文化人群可能是由新来的 印欧人和欧洲土著混合而成。但是 我们认为印欧人是这个新社会的 主要成员，因为他们的语言取代了 欧洲本土的语言。

paragraphs.en: dominant members of this new society, because their language replaced the indigenous European languages. This Corded Ware culture lasted for about 600 years from 2900 to 2300 BC. Amajor cultural change appears to occur with the arrival of the Indo-European Corded Ware culture and the way people are buried undergoes a drastic shift. Among the Funnel-beaker culture, the tradition was to build a passage tomb and bury everybody together inside it. These common tombs seem to indicate a cultural emphasis of the group over the individual. When you died your bones were together with the bones of your ancestors and the rest of your
paragraphs.zh: 这种绳纹器文化繁盛于公元前 2900-2300 年，持续了大约600 年。 随着印欧绳纹器文化的到来，一项 重要的文化变化随之而来。人们被 埋葬的方式发生了巨大的转变。 在漏斗杯文化的传统中，每个人死 后都会被埋葬到通道墓中。这样的 墓葬文明强调集体大过个人。当你 死后，你的尸骨会和祖先、社群内 其他人埋在一起。

paragraphs.en: community. But the Corded Ware culture would bury people separately as individuals, and if the person was a powerful man or woman, he or she would be buried with their valuable possessions and then a small hill, called a "kurgan" would be made above their grave. Perhaps this change in burial practice shows us a shift from communal values to individualistic values with the arrival of the Indo-Europeans. Unfortunately, this Indo-European migration seems also to have been associated with increasing levels of violence. One tragic example of this comes from these four skeletons that were discovered in 2004.
paragraphs.zh: 但是，在绳纹器文化中，人死后会 被单独埋葬。如果是生前有权势的 人，还会有其财产陪葬。人们会在 他/她的墓穴上修建一个名为“库 尔干”的坟头。 这种葬礼方式的改变表明，随着印 欧人的到来，当时的价值观从公共 价值观转向个人主义价值观。 不幸的是，印欧移民似乎也与不断 增加的暴力冲突有关。 一个悲剧性的例子来自于 2004 年 发现的这四具骨架。

paragraphs.en: The skeletons were a family. In fact, this is the oldest nuclear family that has been discovered. They are a father, a mother, and two boys, all buried together. These people belonged to the Corded Ware culture. Sadly, we think they were all killed together when an enemy attacked their home, probably because they had good farmland. Then somebody buried them together. The boys were only 5 and 9 years old when they were murdered. As the Indo-Europeans pushed further west their culture gradually changed. This next culture is called the Bell-Beaker culture, because the pottery has a distinctive bell-shape. As happened further east with the Corded Ware culture, we think that
paragraphs.zh: 这些尸骨是一个家庭。事实上，这 是迄今发现最古老的核心家庭，由 一个父亲，一个母亲，两个男孩组 成。他们被埋葬在一起。这些人属 于绳纹器文化。 不幸的是，我们认为他们都是在敌 人袭击他们的家园时被杀害的，可 能是因为他们有更好的耕地资源。 然后有人把他们埋在了一起。这两 个男孩在被谋杀时分别只有 5 岁 和 9 岁。 随着印欧人向西推进，他们的文化 也逐渐发生了变化。下一种文化被 称为“钟杯”文化，因为其陶器有 着独特的钟形。 随着东部的绳纹器文化逐步发展， 我们认为有一个新的民族诞生了。

paragraphs.en: a new people were created. Genetically these people were a mixture of the original Europeans and the new Indo-Europeans. But due to their greater size and numbers from milk drinking, and their skills with horses, we think that the Indo-Europeans were much more powerful, and so they became culturally dominant. The Bell-Beaker culture lasted from 2900 to 1800 BC, which makes it contemporary with the Old Kingdom of Egypt, theAncient Sumerians, the Indus Valley Civilization, and the Xia Dynasty of China. During the time of the Bell-Beaker culture, the BronzeAge reached Europe. As with copper and agriculture, it began in southern
paragraphs.zh: 从基因上来说，他们是欧洲土著和 印欧移民的混血。 但是由于他们的体型和数量都由 于饮用牛奶和驯马的技能，我们认 为印欧人的力量要强大得多，因此 他们在文化上占主导地位。 钟 杯 文 化 繁 盛 于 公 元 前 2900-1800 年，也就是说，它和埃 及古王国时期、古代苏美尔、印度 河流域文明以及中国的夏朝处在 同一时期。 钟杯文化时期，欧洲进入了青铜时 代。与铜和农业一样，青铜时代也 从欧洲中南部开始，在接下来的几 个世纪里传播到北部和西部。

paragraphs.en: Europe and the Middle East and then spread north and west over several centuries. The entire process took over 2000 years from 3800 to 1500 BC. During the Middle BronzeAge, the Indo-Europeans were the dominant group in Europe, but they were also dividing among themselves, and the original Proto-Indo European language was evolving into many different daughter languages. At the same time, Europe was divided into many different Bronze Age cultures.Again we do not have time to get into details, but some of the broader groups included the Atlantic Bronze Age cultures, Apennine Culture, Terramare Culture, Tumulus Culture, the Nordic BronzeAge, the Unetice
paragraphs.zh: 整个过程从公元前 3800 到公元前 1500 年，持续了超过 2000 年。 青铜时代中期，印欧语在欧洲占主 导地位，但其内部也开始逐渐分 裂，原始印欧语开始细分成诸多派 生语言。 与此同时，欧洲被划分为许多不同 的青铜时代文化。我们没有时间细 讲细节，但可以将其广泛地归类 为：大西洋青铜时代文化，亚平宁 文化，泰拉马拉文化，古墓文化， 北欧青铜时代，乌奈蒂斯文化，以 及诸多巴尔干文化。

paragraphs.en: tradition, and different Balkan Cultures. One of the greatest artifacts of this time period comes from the Unetice Culture, and it is the Nebra Sky disk. The artifact was originally discovered by looters in 1999 who were illegally looking for artifacts to sell. They discovered the sky disk with a metal detector and sold it onto the black market. But in 2002 the looters were arrested by the German police, and the disk was recovered. In order to get a more lenient sentence the looters led the police to the location where they had discovered the artifact. Further excavation at that location turned up bronze swords, chisels,
paragraphs.zh: 这一时期最伟大的作品之一，是来 自于乌奈蒂斯文化的内布拉星象 盘。 该艺术品最初是在 1999 年被劫掠 者发现的，他们非法寻找艺术品来 出售。 他们用金属探测器发现了星象盘， 然后把它卖到了黑市上。但在2002 年，抢掠者被德国警方逮捕，星象 盘被找回。为了得到宽大处理，抢 掠者将警方带到他们发现文物的 地方。 在对遗址进一步的挖掘中，还发现 了青铜剑、凿子、斧头和手镯。所

paragraphs.en: axe-heads and bracelets.All of these artifacts were made around 1600 BC, which is the same age as the Chinese Oracle Bones. The Nebra Sky Disc is such a remarkable artifact that I chose it as the title image for this entire lecture series. Archaeologists speculate that the sky disk may have been used as an astronomical instrument with religious significance. The disk was later given its own permanent exhibit at the State Museum of Prehistory in Halle, Germany. The final stage of the Bronze Age lasted from around 1100 to 500 BC. The European cultures at the time can be broadly divided into the Atlantic Bronze Age system, the
paragraphs.zh: 有这些史前古器物都是在公元前 1600 年左右制成的，与中国的甲 骨文处在同一时期。 内布拉星象盘是一件非常了不起 的作品，因此我选择将它作为这一 课的标题图片。 考古学家推测，星象盘可能被当做 天文仪器使用，并且具有一定的宗 教意义。 该星象盘后来在德国哈雷的史前 历史博物馆作永久性展览。 青铜时代的最后阶段从公元前 1100 年持续到公元前 500 年。当 时的欧洲文化可以大致分为大西 洋青铜时代、瓮棺文化、北欧青铜 时代，以及南部的迈锡尼希腊人。

paragraphs.en: Urnfield System, the Nordic Bronze Age, and in the south, the Mycenean Greeks. All of these people spoke Indo-European languages, and all of them had Bronze metal technology. But writing had not yet reached this part of the world, so Europe was still in the pre-history period, even while literate cultures had developed in the Middle East. Of all these BronzeAge European peoples, the ones we understand the best are the Greeks. But before we talk about the Greeks we need to talk about another civilization which pre-dates them by several centuries: The Minoans. So please join me next time as we shift our focus back south to the
paragraphs.zh: 所有这些人都说印欧语，他们都掌 握着青铜技术。 但文字还没有发明，因此欧洲还处 在史前时代，而中东已经开始发展 起了文学。 在所有这些青铜时代的欧洲人当 中，我们最了解的是希腊人。 但在我们谈论希腊之前，我们需要 谈论另一个文明，它在几个世纪前 就已经存在了：米诺斯文明。 所以，下次当我们把注意力转移到 地中海和克里特岛时，请和我一起

paragraphs.en: Mediterranean and the Isle of Crete, and we learn about the first Literate Society in Europe. Thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 了解欧洲第一个有文化的社会。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第十三讲：“迷宫”里的克里特文明

paragraphs.en: Hello, and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today we are going to talk about a very interesting society known as the Minoans. In terms of our timeline, this culture thrived from 2000 until about 1400 BC. If you remember our class on Neolithic Europe, you will recall that I talked about Middle Eastern Farmers crossing theAegean Sea and bringing farming to Southern Europe around 6000 BC. Farming settlements like Nea Nikomedia, Sesklo & Dimmini, Athens and the Franchthi Caves all began around this time. But this development did not just
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。今天我们来聊聊一个有 趣的古人类文明——米诺斯文明。 从时间线上可以看出，这个文明主 要是在公元前 2000-1400 年之间 发展的。 如果你还记得我们关于欧洲新石 器时代的课程，那么对于我说过的 在公元前 6000 年左右中东农民穿 越爱琴海，把农业带到欧洲南部的 这件事，你一定不陌生。 像尼科美迪亚, 塞斯克利&迪米 尼，雅典和弗兰克西洞穴这样的农 耕群落都是在这个时期开始出现 的。 不只在希腊大陆，这些最古老的农

paragraphs.en: happen on mainland Greece. The earliest farming settlements also began to develop on the Island of Crete. One of the oldest of these settlements is called "Kephala", or at least that's the modern name. I've included a picture of the hill Kephala is located near today, and a model of what it might have looked like at the very beginning of farming on Crete. As happened elsewhere in the world, farming increased the food supply, and over the next 4000 years the population of Crete grew. These early settlements eventually became proper towns. Here is a topographic map of Crete. And you can see the island is made up of a mix of mountains and plains.
paragraphs.zh: 耕群落在克里特岛也开始出现了。 这些群落中最古老的一个被现代 人称为“卡哈那”。我还附上了一 张卡哈那山地区的现状图片，以及 克里特岛上的一个早期农耕群落 的模型。 和世界各地一样，农业的发展增加 了食物供应，在 4000 多年里，克 里特岛的人口不断增长。这些早期 的定居点最终成为了有一定规模 的城镇。 这是克里特岛的地形图。可以看 到，这个岛屿是由山脉和平原组成 的。

paragraphs.en: There are three major mountain regions: The White Mountains in the west, Mount Ida in the center, and the Dikti Mountains in the east. Crete has a warm and humid climate, which is quite good for growing food. The first farmers on Crete did not need to adapt their Middle-Eastern techniques like they did in northern Europe, instead they could start farming almost immediately. This gave Crete a head start in its development. Pretty soon the island was covered with dozens of small farming villages. I think this picture should give you an idea of what Crete feels like on the ground. You can see the flat land in the foreground and Mount Ida rising up in the distance.
paragraphs.zh: 岛上由西部的怀特山脉，中部的伊 达山，东部的迪克提山脉这三个较 大的山区组成。 克里特岛气候温暖潮湿，非常适合 种植粮食。和北欧人的处境不同， 克里特岛上的早期农耕者可以直 接应用来自中东地区的农业技术。 他们可以随时开展耕种。这使克里 特岛在发展中得到了先机。 很快，岛上就被数十个小村庄所覆 盖。 我想这张照片应该能让你对克里 特岛有身临其境的感受。你可以看 到前景中的平原和远处的伊达山。

paragraphs.en: In fact, in Greek Mythology, a cave on Mount Ida was believed to be the place where Rhea hid the infant Zeus to keep him from being swallowed by his father Cronus. But of course, Crete is also an island, and so the ocean has always been very important to the people who lived there.And the island of Crete has many excellent natural harbors. So I think this picture is nice because it also gives you a sense of the importance of the ocean to the people of Crete. The inhabitants of Crete became skilled sailors, traders, and fisherman. In fact, according to the Greek Historian Thucydides, it was the people of Crete who build the first
paragraphs.zh: 在希腊神话中，众神之母瑞亚正是 将婴儿宙斯藏在伊达山上的一个 洞穴里，以免宙斯被他的父亲克洛 诺斯一口吞下。 当然，克里特岛是一个海岛，所以 一直以来海洋对当地居民来说都 是非常重要的。克里特岛有许多天 然的良港。 所以我觉得，这张照片能够让你们 感受到海洋对于克里特岛人民的 重要性。 克里特岛的居民也成为了熟练的 水手、商人和渔夫。 根据希腊历史学家修昔底德的说 法，正是克里特人建立了地中海历 史上的第一支海军。

paragraphs.en: navy in Mediterranean history. By 3500 BC some of the Crete Farming villages had grown into substantial towns.And the chiefs of the bigger towns became rulers over the smaller towns. Crete became a land of small Kingdoms and cities. The people of Crete also raised sheep, and made cloth from the wool. They grew olive orchards and made olive oil. They became skilled at metalworking in bronze, gold and silver. The language of Crete has been lost to us, so we don't know what these first farmers called themselves. But Greek tradition says that Crete was ruled by a Legendary King named
paragraphs.zh: 到公元前 3500 年，克里特岛上的 一些村庄已经发展成为富足的城 镇。大城镇的首领成为小城镇的统 治者。克里特岛出现了小国和城 邦。 克里特人也牧羊，并用羊毛制作衣 物。 他们种植橄榄来制作橄榄油。 他们善于用铜、金、银制作金属器 件。 克里特岛的语言已经失传，所以我 们不知道这些早期农耕者是怎么 称呼自己的。但在希腊传说中克里 特岛是由宙斯的儿子——米诺斯 国王统治的。

paragraphs.en: Minos, who was a son of Zeus. If you've ever read the Myth of Theseus and the Minotaur, King Minos is the wicked King of Crete in that story. Since we do not have another name for these people, archaeologists began calling them the "Minoans" after the legendary King Minos. So that's what we call them from now on. The biggest town on the island was Knossos. The Minoans continued to develop as a culture, by 2700 BC, they had entered the BronzeAge, and by 2000 BC they had developed an advanced society that relied on long-distance trade. Their culture influenced the whole
paragraphs.zh: 如果你读过提修斯和弥诺陶洛斯 的神话，你会知道故事里米诺斯国 王正是统治克里特岛的暴君。 由于不知道这些人的名字，考古学 家便以传说中的米诺斯国王来称 呼他们， “米诺斯人”因此而得 名。 岛上最大的城镇是克诺索斯。 米诺斯人的文化持续发展，早在公 元前 2700 年，他们就进入了青铜 时代，到了公元前 2000 年，他们 已经发展出了一个依靠于长途贸 易的先进社会文明。 他们的文化影响了整个爱琴海地

paragraphs.en: Aegean, and they had a trade network that connected them to Mainland Greece, the Cyclades islands, Cyprus, Syria,Anatolia, Egypt, Mesopotamia, and Spain. The Minoans founded colonies on the Cylcadic Islands, Kethera, Thera, Rhodes and the Mainland. Eventually, Minoan society came to be dominated by several palaces located at the major towns of Knossos, Phaestos, and Malia. The biggest of these was the palace of Knossos. For this reason, historians call the period from 2000 - 1700 BC the Old Palace Period. The palace seems to have been the center of political, religious, and economic power. Thousands of people lived in or around these
paragraphs.zh: 区，他们通过贸易网络将自己与希 腊大陆、基克拉迪斯群岛、塞浦路 斯、叙利亚、安纳托利亚、埃及、 美索不达米亚和西班牙连通起来。 米诺斯人在塞尔卡迪奇群岛、基塞 拉岛、锡拉岛、罗德斯岛和欧洲大 陆都建立了殖民地。 最终，米诺斯社会被首府位于克诺 索斯、费斯托斯和玛利亚等几座大 城镇的宫殿所统治。其中最大的是 克诺索斯宫。 因 此 ， 历 史 学 家 把 公 元 前 2000-1700 年的这段时期称为旧 宫时期。 这座宫殿一直是政治、宗教和经济 的权力中心。成千上万的人住在这 些宫殿周围。这些宫殿也都是贸易 和经济生产的中心。

paragraphs.en: palaces. These were centers of trade and economic production. Let's look at the palace at Knossos in more detail, since it was the largest and is the best studied. The Knossos Palace covers 3 acres and is part of a city which has only been partially excavated. The palace is made of wood with columns, rafters, brushwood on-top of the rafters covered with clay to make a roof. The palace at Knossos was a dense maze of rooms and courtyards where people lived, worked, and stored goods. Windows had columns in the middle which allowed them to include shutters that could be opened up in nice weather.
paragraphs.zh: 让我们来细看克诺索斯的宫殿，它 的规模最大，人们对它的研究也最 深入。 克诺索斯宫殿占地 3 英亩，还是这 座城市被挖掘出来的一部分。 宫殿是用木柱与木椽建造的，房顶 则用粘土覆盖在木排上制成。 克诺索斯宫简直是一个密布的迷 宫，里面包含有许多房间和庭院， 人们在这里生活、工作和储存物 资。 窗户中间的木柱用来安装百叶窗， 可以在天气好的时候打开使用。

paragraphs.en: The Minoan column (inverted column) is wider at the top than the bottom.And they are usually painted red black and yellow. (They are basically stylized tree trunks.) In Minoan art, women are often depicted as wearing clothes that reveal their breasts. This is very different from the dress of the mainland Greeks who came later, and perhaps it reflects differences in the status of women in Minoan society. Perhaps the most impressive feature of the palace was the plumbing system. The Minoan palaces had hot and cold running water! Water was carried to and from the palace along interlocking clay pipes. The palace also had basic flush toilets. This was
paragraphs.zh: 米诺斯柱(倒置柱)的上部比下部 粗。人们通常将它们涂成红色、黑 色和黄色。(它们都是统一规格的 树干。) 在米诺斯艺术中，图画中的女人通 常穿着露出胸部的衣物。这与后来 出现的希腊大陆人的服装大不相 同，也许这反映了女性在米诺斯社 会中的不同地位。 宫殿最令人印象深刻的，也许就是 管道系统了。 米诺斯的宫殿里有冷热的自来水！ 水流通过首尾连接的陶制管道进 出宫殿。宫殿里还有基本的冲水厕 所。这是世界上最早的室内管道工 程。而这种生活水平在未来一千年

paragraphs.en: the earliest indoor-plumbing in the world.And this standard of living would not be reached again for over a thousand years. The various palaces were linked by stone-faced roads. The road linking Knossos to its port ofAmnisos is the oldest road in Europe. As the Minoans society grew in complexity, they needed a better system of record keeping to keep track of all the items that they were producing and trading. Perhaps inspired by their trade partners in Egypt, the Minoans developed their own form of picture writing around 1900 BC, which is known as Minoan Hieroglyphics. They most famous example of this is the Phaistos disk.
paragraphs.zh: 之内都没有文明能够超越。 各宫殿之间由石砌道路相连。 这条欧洲最古老的道路连接了克 诺索斯和姆尼索斯港。 随着米诺斯社会的日渐发展，人们 需要更好的方法来记录他们生产 和贸易的物品。 也许是受他们埃及贸易伙伴的启 发，米诺斯人在公元前 1900 年左 右发展出了自己用图画记载的形 式，这被称为米诺斯象形文字。最 著名的例子就是费斯托斯圆盘。

paragraphs.en: But this hierolgyphic system was probably too slow and cumbersome, so by 1800 BC, the Minoans had developed a 2nd writing system that was faster and easier to write. Since the symbols involve a lot of straight lines, this writing system is called LinearA. Unfortunately, we do not know how to read either Minoan Hieroglyphics or Linear A.And so, although we know that Minoans had a writing system, the knowledge contained in those symbols remains lost to us. The Minoans also created great art like this painting, called "The Ladies in Blue" The Minoans loved to add paint to plaster to make frescoes. They loved to paint nature and marine life.
paragraphs.zh: 但是这种象形文字系统可能书写 太慢太麻烦，所以在公元前 1800 年，米诺斯人发展出了新的文字系 统，速度更快，更容易书写。 因为这些符号包含很多直线，所以 这种文字被称做线性文字 A。 遗憾的是，我们现在无法读懂米诺 斯象形文字或线形文字 A。因此， 尽管我们知道米诺斯人曾经有过 文字，但这些符号中所包含的信息 对我们来说已经遗失在历史长河 中了。 米诺斯人也创造了伟大的艺术，例 如像这幅叫做“蓝衣女子”的画。 米诺斯人喜欢将颜料涂在灰泥上 来制作壁画。他们热衷于描绘自然 和海洋生物。

paragraphs.en: Their paintings are devoid of war scenes and the people in them are usually depicted as happy, young and graceful. Perhaps this indicates that Minoan life was peaceful compared to other early cultures, and their artwork celebrated life and nature. Two other famous Minoan frescos are this one called the blue monkeys and this one showing two boys boxing. Nature and Youth are frequently celebrated in Minoan artwork. The Minoans also seem to have had a rich religious tradition. But because none of their writing survives, we can only guess at the details. The Minoans did seem to have a
paragraphs.zh: 他们的画作里没有战争场面，画中 的人物往往年轻、快乐而优雅。 也许这表明，与其他早期文化相 比，米诺斯人的生活是和平的，他 们的艺术作品颂扬生命和自然。 还有两幅著名的米诺斯壁画，一幅 画叫做蓝猴子，另一幅则描绘了两 个男孩拳击的场面。 米诺斯的艺术作品经常歌颂自然 和青春。 米诺斯人似乎也有丰富的宗教传 统。但由于他们的文字没有留存， 我们只能猜测其中的细节。 米诺斯人似乎对公牛有极大的崇

paragraphs.en: great reverence for and fascination with Bulls. Perhaps this is the origin of the legend of the Minotaur. One of their religious rituals seems to involve the sport of Bull leaping. Ayoung man would grab the horns of a charging bull and throw himself over its back. The youth could demonstrate his strength, agility and courage, perhaps as part of a religious ritual. Here is a Minoan Fresco depicting that ritual. And here is an illustration of the same ritual to give you a more vivid impression of what it may have been like. The Minoans, like many early agriculturalists, had great reverence for Bulls. They seemed to be popular symbols of the power of
paragraphs.zh: 敬和迷恋。也许这就是牛头人传说 的起源。 他们的一项宗教仪式里有跳跃公 牛的运动。一个年轻人会抓住一头 冲过来的公牛的角，然后扑到它的 背上。也许是作为宗教仪式的一部 分，这个年轻人可以展示他的力 量，敏捷度和勇气。 这幅米诺斯壁画描绘了这个场景。 这里有一张宗教仪式的示意图，你 可以从中对此有一个更生动的印 象。 米诺斯人和许多早期的农耕者一 样，对牛怀有极高的崇敬之情。牛 似乎象征着大自然的力量。

paragraphs.en: nature. This ritual was probably conducted in or near the palace, which really was the center of all economic, political and cultural life. Because their language is lost to us, we can only guess at the meaning of many part of Minoan culture. But we think that part of their religious worship was held at sacred caves across Crete, and the bare-chested female figurine holding the snakes might be a Minoan goddess. Other than Knossos, the other very important Minoan archaeological site is calledAkrotini. This town was a Minoan trading colony located on the Island of Thera, which is called Santorini today. Here is a map showing you where Akrotini was located and an
paragraphs.zh: 这一仪式可能是在宫殿内或附近 举行的，因为宫殿是所有经济、政 治和文化生活的中心。 因为无法解读他们的语言，我们只 能猜测米诺斯文化各方面的意义。 我们认为他们有的宗教仪式是在 克里特岛的神圣洞穴里举行的，而 那个赤裸上身，手里举着蛇的女性 雕像可能是米诺斯文化里的女神。 除了克诺索斯之外，另一个非常重 要的米诺斯人遗址叫做阿克罗蒂 里。这个小镇是米诺斯人的贸易殖 民地，位于锡拉岛，也就是今天的 圣托里尼岛。 从地图上可以看到阿克罗蒂里的 所在地，这里还有这个小镇的示意

paragraphs.en: illustration of the town. We have also found a Minoan fresco which might depict the town of Akrotini as it appeared in its heyday. Unfortunately for the Minoans at Akrotini, Thera is a volcano, and around the year 1590 BC, it erupted. Fortunately, it seems that the people ofAkrotini escaped in time because we have found no skeletons among the ruins of the town, but we have found many valuable items that people probably had to abandon it in a hurry. The eruption buried the town in 50 meters of ash, which preserved it in a pristine condition for later archeologists. That is why we have so many good Minoan artifacts from Akrotini.
paragraphs.zh: 图。 我们还发现了一幅米诺斯人的壁 画，描绘的可能是阿克罗蒂里的全 盛时期。 不幸的是，锡拉岛是一座火山岛， 大约在公元前 1590 年，它爆发了。 幸运的是，阿克罗蒂里人似乎及时 逃离了，因为我们在这座城镇的废 墟中没有发现任何骨骸，但我们发 现了许多贵重的物品，人们可能是 在匆忙之中离开的。 火山爆发将这座城镇埋在了 50 米 深的火山灰中，为后来的考古学家 保存了其最原始的状态。这也是我 们能够从阿克罗蒂里得到这么多 保存完好的米诺斯文物的原因。

paragraphs.en: But this was a huge eruption. This is a picture of the island of Thera today.As you can see there is a giant bay filled with ocean water in the center of the island. But that Bay did not exist until 3500 years ago when the eruption literally blew a large chunk of the island into the ocean. You can see that in this illustration. When a volcano blasts a large amount of rock into the ocean all at once, this can sometimes generate a tsunami, and that is what happened after the Thera eruption. Agiant wave, perhaps 20 meters high, raced across the Eastern Mediterranean at the speed of a jet airplane. The wave hit Knossos within 20
paragraphs.zh: 但这是一场巨大的火山喷发。从塞 拉岛现在的照片可以见到，岛的中 心形成了一个巨大的海湾，里面全 是海水。 但是个海湾是 3500 年前才出现 的，那时火山爆发甚至将岛的大部 分土地都喷入了大海。 你可以在示意图中看到这个过程。 当火山喷发突然向海洋中冲入大 量岩块时，可能会引发海啸，图中 正是锡拉岛火山爆发后发生的情 况。 一股 20 米高的巨浪以飞机一般的 速度掠过地中海东部。 火山爆发后 20 分钟内，巨浪袭击

paragraphs.en: minutes of the eruption. It hit Cyprus after an hour and slammed into the Egyptian and Israel coasts 90 minutes after the explosion. The Tsunami destroyed the port of Knossos and much of the Minoan fleet. If you have ever seen video footage of the 2011 Tsunami which struck Japan, you should imagine something similar happening to northern Crete. Furthermore, the ash and dust from the eruption probably damaged that year's crops, leading to a famine. And then the volcano probably caused many years of strange weather, which made the situation even worse. Many ancient Myths like the war between the Titans and the Gods, or the Lost City ofAtlantis, may have
paragraphs.zh: 了克诺索斯。爆发一小时后，它袭 击了塞浦路斯，90 分钟后又袭击 了埃及和以色列海岸。 海啸摧毁了克诺索斯港口和许多 米诺安的船只。如果你看过 2011 年东日本海啸的视频，你应该想象 一下类似的事情发生在克里特岛 北部是怎样的场景。 此外，火山喷发出的火山灰和尘土 可能会毁坏庄稼地，导致饥荒。 火山还可能造成多年的气候异常， 令情况雪上加霜。 许多古老的神话，如泰坦与诸神之 间的战争，或失落的亚特兰蒂斯 城，都可能起源于这一历史事件。

paragraphs.en: their origin in this historical event. After the eruption on Thera, Minoan society went into a long, slow decline over the next 150 years.A new people, the Mycenean Greeks, had moved into theAegean and begun to compete with the Minoans. Around 1450 BC, the Myceneans invaded Crete. The burned the towns of Phaestos, Zagros and Minos, but Knossos they kept for themselves. This invasion marks the end of an Independent Minoan culture. They had been replaced by a new-comer, the Mycenean Greeks, who were an Indo European people just like the Hittites. So please join me next time as we learn about these new-comers, the Mycenean Greeks.
paragraphs.zh: 锡拉岛火山爆发后，米诺斯文明在 接下来的 150 年里经历了漫长而 缓慢的衰退。一个新的民族——希 腊的迈锡尼人，已经进入爱琴海， 并开始与米诺斯人竞争。 大约在公元前 1450 年，迈锡尼人 入侵了克里特岛。他们烧毁了法埃 斯托斯、扎格罗斯和米诺斯的城 镇，但他们将克诺索斯留作己用。 这次入侵标志着克里特文明的终 结。取而代之的，是新出现的希腊 迈锡尼人，他们和赫梯人一样是印 欧人。 下节课，我们将会一起了解这个新 民族——希腊迈锡尼人。

paragraphs.en: I hope you have enjoyed this lesson, and I have some recommendations for you if you want to learn more. First of all, I'd like to make it clear for students WHERE Crete is. It is the biggest Island in theAegean Sea, and today it is part of the country of Greece. Here is Greece on a map of Europe and here is Crete on a map of Greece. Knossos is located on the northern side of the island, a few miles inland from the coast. It is actually very close to Crete's main city of Heraklion. The Knossos dig site was first excavated by a British archaeologist named Arthur Evans in 1900. He rebuilt part of the palace to give visitors a better idea of what it looked like during the Minoan era.
paragraphs.zh: 我希望你喜欢这节课，如果你想更 深入地学习，我可以给你一些建 议。 首先，我想让同学们弄清楚克里特 岛在哪里。它是爱琴海最大的岛 屿，现在是希腊的一部分。这是欧 洲地图上的希腊，以及希腊地图上 的克里特岛。 克诺索斯位于岛的北部，离海岸只 有几英里。实际上，它离克里特岛 的主要城市伊拉克利翁非常近。 克诺索斯遗址在 1900 年由一位名 叫阿瑟·埃文斯的英国考古学家首 次发掘。他重建了一部分宫殿，来 让游客更好地了解克里特文明。这 就是部分废墟被粉刷和修复的原 因。

paragraphs.en: This explains the painted and restored part of the ruins. If you are curious to learn more, then I highly recommend The History of Ancient Greece podcast by Ryan Stitt and the book The Greeks written by Roy Burrell and Peter Connolly. I had that book as a boy and I loved it. The best museum to go to if you want to sea these Minoan artifacts in person is the Heraklion Archeological Museum in Heraklion Crete. It is there that you can see the Bull Leaping fresco, the Ladies in Blue and the Phaistos Disk. The other great museum is the archaeological site ofAkrotiri on the Island of Santorini. There you can see the preserved Minoan town for yourself.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你想了解更多，我强烈推荐 Ryan Stitt 的《古希腊历史播客》 和罗伊·伯勒尔和彼得· 康诺利 的《希腊人》一书。我小时候就读 过这本书，非常喜欢。 如果你想亲自看看文物，最推荐的 博物馆就是克里特岛上的伊拉克 利翁考古博物馆。在那里，你可以 看到跳跃公牛的壁画，穿蓝衣服的 女人和费斯托斯圆盘。 另一个不错的博物馆是圣托里尼 岛上的阿克罗蒂里考古遗址。在那 里你可以看到保存完好的米诺斯 城镇。

paragraphs.en: Thank you very much and I'll see you next time.
paragraphs.zh: 谢谢大家，下次再见。

titleZh: 第十四讲：带着黄金面具的迈锡尼文明

paragraphs.en: Hello, and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today we are going to be talking about the people who conquered the Minoans, the Mycenaean Greeks. But we won't just talk about them, we will also look at some of the other people who lived near them around theAegean Sea: The Hittites and the Trojans. On our timeline, we will be looking at 800 years of history, from about 2000 BC until 1200 BC, which also marks the end of the BronzeAge, and the beginning of a Dark age in Western History. Here is what that looks like on a timeline: The story of the Greeks begins
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。今天我们要讲的，是在 上一节课中征服了米诺斯文明的 民族，迈锡尼希腊人。但我们的目 光将不限于此：我们之后还要去看 看同样生活在爱琴海周边地区的 赫梯人和特洛伊人。 我 们 将 回 顾 时 间 线 上 公 元 前 2000-1200年之间这段800年的历 史，在此期间，青铜时代告一段落， 西方历史上的一个黑暗时代开始 了。 我们来看看时间线： 公元前 2000 年左右，一个说印欧

paragraphs.en: around 2000 BC.Atribe of people who spoke an Indo-European language move south into Greece from the Balkans. These people were the descendants to the Yamna herders who we learned about a few lessons ago, and we know this because their language, archaic Greek, belongs to the Indo-European family of languages. These people were warlike herders; they had bronze weapons; and they were masters of the horse and wagon. These Indo-Europeans became the dominant group in terms of language and culture, but they probably intermarried with the local people as well.And since Greece is mountainous, unlike their Steppe
paragraphs.zh: 语系的部落从巴尔干半岛向南进 入了希腊，古希腊人的故事由此开 始。 这些人是我们在几节课前学过的 亚穆纳草原民族的后代，之所以知 道这一点，是因为他们的语言也是 古希腊语，同属于印欧语系。 这些人非常好战；他们用青铜制造 武器；同时也是骑马和驾驭马车的 高手。 这些印欧人在语言和文化上占据 了主体地位，但他们也可能与原住 民通婚。希腊不同于他们之前生活 的的草原地区，是一个多山的国 家，因此早期的古希腊人也学会了 耕种、捕鱼和航海技术。

paragraphs.en: homelands, these early Greeks also learned how to farm, fish and sail. By the 1600s BC, a new culture had emerged in the southern Peloponnesian Peninsula. It was a mixture of the original people and the Indo-European new-comers. We call these people the Mycenaean Greeks. For a few hundred years, they were less powerful and sophisticated than the Minoan civilization which dominated the area. But after the eruption of Thera around 1590 BC, the Minoans began to weaken, and the Mycenaean Greeks began to take their place. And then in the 1400s the Mycenaeans were strong enough to invade Crete and conquer the Minoans.
paragraphs.zh: 到公元前 17 世纪，在南伯罗奔尼 撒半岛出现了一个新的民族。它是 原住民和外来的印欧人的融合体。 我们称他们为迈锡尼希腊人。 几百年来，他们一直不如米诺斯文 明强大和先进。但在公元前 1590 年锡拉岛火山爆发后，米诺斯文明 开始衰退，迈锡尼人逐渐取代了他 们的地位。 到了公元前 15 世纪，迈锡尼人已 经足够强大，他们入侵克里特岛， 征服了米诺斯文明。

paragraphs.en: From 1400 to 1100 BC, all of this area in pink was part of Mycenaean culture. We don't think the Mycenaeans just had one king; they were probably dis-unified politically. BUT they did share a common culture and language. The Mycenaeans also continued to learn from the more advanced Minoan culture. We can see Minoan influence in their building designs and their artwork. But the biggest Minoan influence was writing. You see, the Mycenaean Greeks did not have a writing system when they arrived in the region. The Indo-European culture was not yet sophisticated enough for that.
paragraphs.zh: 在公元前 1400-1100 年之间，图中 粉红色的区域都属于迈锡尼文化 的一部分。 我们认为这片区域由多个国家组 成；迈锡尼人很可能在政治上不统 一。但他们确实拥有共同的文化和 语言。 迈锡尼人一直在向更先进的米诺 斯文明学习。我们可以从他们的建 筑设计和艺术作品中看到米诺斯 文明的影子。 但米诺斯文明带来的最大的影响 是文字。 迈锡尼人刚踏上这片土地时，由于 印欧文化还不够成熟，他们并没有 文字系统。

paragraphs.en: But once they settled down, the Greeks saw the advantage of literacy, and so they borrowed the Minoan system of LinearA, and then they adopted it to their own language. This first Greek writing system is called Linear B by archaeologists, in order to separate it from the Minoan LinearA. Like Cuneiform or Hieroglyphics, Linear B WAS NOT an alphabet. Instead it was a collection of about 200 symbols. About half of the symbols indicated sounds, but these sounds were full syllables. So these symbols were syllabograms. They indicated sounds like "ad" or "ma" or "ro." And these sounds could be put together to make any word the
paragraphs.zh: 但是在这里，他们认识到拥有读写 能力所带来的便利，所以他们借鉴 了米诺斯的线性文字 A 以应用到 自己的语言中。 为了与米诺斯的线性文字 A 区分 开来，考古学家们将这个最早期的 古希腊文字系统称为线性文字 B。 线性文字 B 和楔形文字、象形文字 一样，并不是一列字母表。它是由 大约 200 个符号组成的。 其中大约半数的符号代表发音，每 个发音都是完整的音节。所以每个 书写符号都代表单独的一个音节。 它们听起来就像 “啊”或“嘛” 或“若”。将这些音节组合起来就 成为了迈锡尼人的语言。

paragraphs.en: Mycenaeans wanted. The other half of the symbols represented ideas that were very important in Mycenaean culture. These were symbols for words like: "man", "woman", "horse", "gold", "wool", "barley" and "chariot". For a long time, archaeologists thought that the Mycanaean's spoke a language other than Greek. They thought that the Greeks were a different people who came later. But in the 1950s, two men were able to decipher Linear B. Their names were Michael Ventris and John Chadwick, and they proved that the Mycaeneans actually spoke an ancient version of Greeks. This proved that they were the ancestors of the classical Greeks who play such a big role in world
paragraphs.zh: 另一半符号则代表了迈锡尼文化 中一些重要的含义。每个符号都是 一个词语，例如:“男人”、“女人”、 “马”、“黄金”、“羊毛”、“大麦” 和“战车”。 在很长一段时间里，考古学家都认 为迈锡尼人说的不是希腊语。他们 认为古希腊人是在迈锡尼人之后 才出现的。 但在 20 世纪 50 年代，迈克尔·文 特里斯和约翰·查德威克成功破译 了线性文字 B。 这一成果证实了在世界历史上有 着重要地位的古希腊人，传承了迈 锡尼人的血脉。

paragraphs.en: history. Interestingly, Michael Ventris was not even a professional archaeologist or linguist, who was just an amateur who was passionate about Linear B and wanted to figure it out. Unfortunately, the Linear B tablets that we have deciphered are not very interesting. They are mostly records for the palaces where the Kings lived. They keep track of materials and work, but don't tell us very much about history or the politics of the time. The greatest of the Mycenaean palaces was the one at Mycenae, so we named the whole culture after it. Greek tradition also tells us that the King of Mycenae was the High-King over all the other Greek
paragraphs.zh: 有趣的是，迈克尔·文特里斯不是 一个专业的考古学家或语言学家， 他只是一个对线性文字 B 充满热 情的业余爱好者。 遗憾的是，在我们目前找到的写有 线性文字 B 的泥板上，还没有发现 太多吸引人的内容。这些文字大部 分都是行政记录，如仓库清点和工 作进度等，没有关于当时的历史或 政治的信息。 迈锡尼古城内有迈锡尼人当时最 宏伟的宫殿，迈锡尼文化因此得 名。 从希腊的传统文化也可以知道，迈 锡尼城统治者的地位远高于希腊 其他地区，这也是我们给“迈锡尼

paragraphs.en: leaders, so that is a second reason we give this stage of Greek civilization the name "Mycenaean." The town of Mycenae still exists, but it is a ruin. Here is what it looks like today. But here is a picture of what it probably looked like in 1300 BC. There are a couple of things I want you to pay attention to. The first is the main gate at the bottom of the picture. This is called the Lion Gate.Also look at the walls. The next is that circle at the bottom right. That is called Grave CircleA. And at the top you can see the palace and the Megaron. We will examine each of these
paragraphs.zh: 人”命名的另一个原因。 迈锡尼古城今天依然存在，但它已 是一片废墟。这是它现在的照片。 这里也有一张迈锡尼古城在公元 前 1300 年时的复原图。 我想让你们留意一下其中的几个 部分。 首先是图中下方的大门。它叫做狮 门。再看看城墙。 接下来是右下角的那个圆形建筑。 它被称为圆形墓葬 A。 在图中的上方你可以看到宫殿和 中央大厅。 我们继续深入了解这些地方的细

paragraphs.en: places in more detail. Mycenae was a good place to have a settlement. It was on a hill that is 300 meters above sea level, so it is easy to defend. There was a good water source nearby.And it was next to a fertile plain that was good for farming. Mycenae was about 19 kilometers from the ocean, which meant that it was close enough for trade, but far enough inland that it would be difficult to attack by surprise. Here is the main gate for Mycenae; notice the carved lions over the entrance. This is why this is called the Lion's gate.Also notice that there are big walls on either side. If you tried to attack the gate, the defenders could shoot at you from both sides.
paragraphs.zh: 节。 迈锡尼城是个宜居之地。它位于海 拔 300 米的山丘上，易守难攻。附 近有良好的水源。山脚下就是一片 肥沃的平原，适合耕种。 迈锡尼城距离海边约 19 公里，与 外界进行贸易比较便利，同时又保 持了足够的安全距离，以防止遭到 敌人的突袭。 这是迈锡尼城的大门；狮门因入口 上方雕刻的狮子而得名。入口两边 都建有高大的城墙。如果你想进攻 大门，守军可以从两边向你射箭。

paragraphs.en: Probably the most impressive thing about Mycenae is the size of the stones in its walls. Building those walls was a huge engineering feat in the second millennium BC, and the stones are so big that a legend developed that they were not built by men, but instead were built by the great Cyclopes. For this reason, these walls are called the "Cyclopean Walls" Mycenae was a very hierarchical place. We know this from the linear B tablets. Usually, only the King and his nobles would live inside the city walls. The common people would only come inside if there was an attack. From the Linear B tablets we know the names for the different classes in
paragraphs.zh: 迈锡尼城内最令人印象深刻的可 能就是造墙用的巨石了。在公元前 2000 年就建造了这些城墙，可以 说是一项伟大的工程壮举。这些石 头如此之大，以至于在传说中它们 是由希腊神话中的独眼巨人建造 的。 因此，这些墙被人们称作“独眼巨 人墙”。 我们从线性文字 B 中了解到，迈锡 尼城等级森严。 正常情况下，只有国王和贵族才可 以住在城墙内。而平民只有当遭到 袭击时才能够进来。 从线性文字 B 中我们还知道了社 会中不同阶层的称谓。

paragraphs.en: society. The head of the society and the leader of the economy was the "Wanax" or King. Beneath him was the "Lagawetas" who was his 2nd in command, and probably the leader of the army. Next came the "Telestai" who were the priests, and the "Hequetas" who were the high-ranking officials.All of these men owned large amounts of land. This land would be worked by the common people or "Demos." Some of the common people owned their own land, or made things, but the rest worked as tenant farmers for the rich. And finally at the bottom of society were the "Doero", the slaves.
paragraphs.zh: 全面领导社会和经济的人叫做“万 纳克斯”，也就是国王。 在他之下的是“拉加韦特”，即国 王的副手，可能是军队的首领。 再过来是“忒列太”和“希克塔”， 前者是祭司，后者是高级官员。这 些人都拥有大量的土地。 而土地则由平民“迪莫斯”来耕种。 一些平民拥有自己的土地，或依靠 制造物品为生，但其余人为富人做 佃农。 最后在社会底层的是奴隶，他们被 称为“多尔诺”。

paragraphs.en: The center of the society was a large room in the palace where the King kept his fire burning. This is where the King met with his advisors and made important decisions. It is where he welcomed guests, and it where he held great feasts. This room is called the "Megaron" and it was the center of Mycenaean society. On the left is a picture of a Megaron, and on the right are the ruins of the actual megaron at Mycenae. Do you remember the circle on that picture of Mycenae, the one called Grave CircleA? Well this was the royal cemetery. Here in the Grave Circle, just inside the city walls, the Mycenaean's
paragraphs.zh: 整个社会的心脏是宫殿里的一个 大厅，国王在这里统治社稷。 国王在这里会见群臣并作出各种 重大决策。这也是他招待客人、举 办盛宴的地方。 这个房间叫做“中央大厅”，是迈 锡尼社会的中心。 左边是中央大厅的复原图，右边则 是迈锡尼城的中央大厅遗址。 你还记得迈锡尼城的那张图片上 叫做圆形墓葬 A 的地方吗?这可是 皇家公墓。 墓地位于城墙内，迈锡尼人挖出深 达 12 米的洞穴。

paragraphs.en: would dig a deep hole, sometimes as much at 12 meters deep. In this hole they would bury their kings with several of their most valuable possessions.And then they would fill the hole back up to prevent grave robbing.And the top of the whole a gravestone would be placed to commemorate the person buried there. These kinds of graves are called "shaft graves", and they were common from around 1650 to 1500 BC. On the left is an illustration of the shaft graves at Grave CircleAin Mycenae, and on the right is a picture of one of those shaft graves, which have been dug up. Here is another picture of Grave CircleAin order to give you a better
paragraphs.zh: 他们会在洞内为国王放入最珍贵 的陪葬品，然后再把洞填满，以防 盗墓。最后人们在顶部会放置墓碑 以作纪念。 这种坟墓被称为“井墓”，它们常 见于公元前 1650-1500 年。 左边是迈锡尼圆形墓葬 A 的示意 图，右边则是一个挖掘出来的墓 穴。 为了让你们对圆形墓葬 A 有更清 晰的印象，这里还有一张图片。

paragraphs.en: picture of what it looks like. Grave CircleAis the royal burial ground and it is located inside of the city walls to protect it from tomb robbers. But thought the Mycenaean kings could protect their graves in their day, they have been unable to protect them in the 3000 years since their civilization collapsed. Some of the graves were plundered by grave robbers. But others have been excavated by archaeologists, and as you can see the tombs were full of beautiful golden objects. The most famous is a death mask for one of the Mycenaean Kings. The quantity and quality of the gold objects is strong evidence that these were the tombs of kings.
paragraphs.zh: 圆形墓葬 A 是皇家墓地，因此它位 于城墙内，以免被盗墓。 虽然迈锡尼王朝的国王可以在他 们的时代里保护好他们的坟墓，但 在他们文明崩溃之后的 3000 年 中，他们对此无能为力。 有些坟墓已经被盗墓者洗劫一空。 但也有一些由考古学家发掘，图中 可以看到，这些坟墓里装满了精美 的黄金制品。 其中最著名的是迈锡尼国王的死 亡面具。 从金器的数量和质量可以推断出 这是国王的坟墓。

paragraphs.en: Then, as the Mycenaean kings became even wealthier they changed their style of tomb. Instead of being buried in a simple tomb, they build large domes out of stone, with stone passageways that lead to the entrance. Then the body was put inside the dome, the treasures were placed next to it, and the entire dome was covered with earth to make an artificial hill. These new types of tombs are called "Tholos Tombs" and they are the pinnacle of Mycenaean engineering. Here on the left is an illustration of one of these "Tholos tombs" and on the right is a picture of the greatest of these tombs, nicknamed "The Treasury ofAtreus".
paragraphs.zh: 随着迈锡尼王朝的发展壮大，他们 的国王改变了墓葬风格。 他们不再建造之前那种简易的坟 墓，而是用石头建造了巨大的圆顶 建筑，以及通往入口的石头通道。 人们把死者与陪葬品安置于圆顶 内之后，会用土覆盖整个建筑，形 成了一座人造的小山。 这种新型的坟墓被称为“圆顶墓”， 它们代表着迈锡尼工程技术的顶 峰。 左边是 “圆顶墓”的示意图，右 边是现存最宏伟的一座，被称为 “阿特罗伊斯的宝库”。

paragraphs.en: Here is picture of the inside of the tomb, to help you get a sense of size. This tomb was actually the largest dome in the world until the Roman period. We think that the Mycenaean Greeks were much more warlike than the Minoans because warfare is a much bigger part of their art. For example, this vase is called the "Warrior Vase" and it is probably the most famous work of Mycenaean pottery. It is a "krater" which is bowl used to mix water with wine. This was viewed as civilized by the Greeks, since they thought it barbaric to drink undiluted wine. As you can see it shows us a group of warriors marching. They are
paragraphs.zh: 这是一张坟墓内部的照片，从中你 可以对其规模有一个大致的了解。 这座墓甚至是罗马时代之前世界 上最大的圆顶建筑。 我们认为迈锡尼人比米诺斯人更 加好战，因为战争是他们艺术表现 中非常重要的一部分。 例如，这个花瓶被称为“战士花 瓶”，它是最著名的迈锡尼陶器作 品。 它是一种“双耳喷口杯”，是一种 用来把水和酒混合的碗。古希腊人 认为这是文明的象征，因为在他们 的观念中，饮用不加稀释的酒是野 蛮的行为。 可以看到，瓶身描绘了是一群战士 行进中的画面。他们携带头盔、盾

paragraphs.en: carrying helmets, shields and spears, and are wearing breast-plate armor. We can also see the Mycenaean obsession with war in the arms and armor that they left behind. On the left I have included a picture of the Dendra Panoply. This is the oldest suit of full-body metal armor in the world. It was made in the 1400s BC. About the same time as the Mycenaean conquest of Crete. Interestingly, the Mycenaean's did not make their helmets out of bronze. They seem to have preferred making them by weaving together the tusks of several boars. You can see a picture of one of these helmets on the right. These were also worn into battle in the 15th century BC.
paragraphs.zh: 牌和长矛，身穿胸甲。 我们也可以看到从迈锡尼人留下 的武器和盔甲中，看出他们对于战 争的狂热。 左边的图片是一张登德拉全身铠 甲的照片，这是世界上最古老的全 身金属盔甲。它是在公元前 15 世 纪制造的。与迈锡尼人征服克里特 岛为同一个时代。 有趣的是，迈锡尼人的头盔并不是 用青铜制作的，而是由数根公猪的 獠牙编织而成。 右边就是一个头盔的照片。它们曾 被用于公元前 15 世纪的战争中。

paragraphs.en: And to help you put it all together in your imagination. Here is an illustration of a Mycenaean soldiers. The man in the center is probably a rich man or a king, since he can afford a full set of metal armor like the panoply. Notice how all the men are wearing boars-tusk helmets. The man with the bronze armor is getting into a chariot. Since the Greeks were Indo-Europeans they had chariots, but they were probably in limited use because of the mountainous terrain of Greece. In the back are ships, because the Greeks were excellent sailors.And on the ground is a oxhide and wooden figure-8 shield, which was common at the time. Now that concludes our introduction
paragraphs.zh: 为了让你有一个完整的印象，来看 看这张关于迈锡尼士兵的插图吧。 坐在中间的可能是个富人或国王， 因为他能拥有全套金属盔甲。 图中的每一个人都戴着猪牙头盔。 身穿铜甲的那个人正在登上战车。 由于迈锡尼人是印欧人种，他们有 使用战车的传统，但在实际应用 中，战车可能会受制于希腊的山区 地形。 从图片远处许多船只可以看出，迈 锡尼人还是优秀的水手。地面上有 当时常用的牛皮和木制的 8 字型 盾牌。 以上就是对迈锡尼希腊人的介绍。

paragraphs.en: to the Mycanaean Greeks. Next we are going to shift our attention across theAegean Sea and look at two other societies that existed at the same time: The Hittites and the Trojans. The Hittites were another Indo-European people, and they were moving into theAnatolian Peninsula at roughly the same time that the Proto-Greeks were moving into the Peloponnesian Peninsula. Next class we we look at these people in much more detail. So I hope you join me for that! I hope you enjoyed that lesson on the Mycenaean Greeks. If you want to learn more, I have some excellent resources to share with you. The first is a museum, one of the greatest in the world for Mycenaean
paragraphs.zh: 接下来我们将把注意力转向爱琴 海的另一头，看看当时与之共存的 另外两个文明:赫梯人和特洛伊 人。 赫梯人是另一个印欧民族，他们进 入安纳托利亚半岛的时间与迈锡 尼人进入伯罗奔尼撒半岛的时间 大致相同。 下节课我们将对这些人有更详细 的介绍。大家不要错过! 希望你们喜欢这节关于迈锡尼人 的课程。如果你还想了解更多，我 有一些资源要与你分享。 首先是博物馆，位于希腊雅典的国 家考古博物馆内收藏有世界上数

paragraphs.en: artifacts is the National Archaeological Museum inAthens Greece. Here you can see the Warrior Vase, a boars-head helmet and the Dendra Panoply. When it comes to other resources, The History of Ancient Greece podcast is fantastic, which I've mentioned before. In Search of the Trojan War by Michael wood is a good book on this topic, which I've read recently. Finally, these are some excellent websites if you want to learn more about the ancient Greeks. And the website facts and details has great information about many different societies. I hope you enjoy them all.
paragraphs.zh: 一数二的迈锡尼文物。在这里你可 以看到战士花瓶，猪牙头盔和登德 拉全身铠甲。 说到其他的资源，有一个叫《古希 腊历史》的播客非常不错，我之前 也和你们提到过。 迈克尔·伍德的《寻找特洛伊战争》 是一本好书，我最近读过。 最后，如果你还想了解更多关于古 希腊的信息，这里有一些不错的网 站。 网站里有很多关于古代社会的信 息。 希望你们会喜欢。

titleZh: 第十五讲：一方霸主赫梯帝国和盟友特洛伊

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today, let's shift our focus to another people who were also Indo-Europeans and lived at the same time as the Mycenaean Greeks. These people were the Hittites. They probably moved south a few hundred years before the Greeks, but the exact time is unknown, probably in the 500 years between 2000 and 1500 BC, but maybe a few centuries earlier than that. Here you can see an approximate map showing the migration of both the Greeks and Hittites from the Indo-European homeland into the
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。 今天，让我们把目光转移到另一个 民族身上。他们是印欧人，与迈锡 尼希腊人处在同一时期。他们就是 赫梯人。 他们可能比希腊人早几百年南迁， 但确切的时间还不清楚，可能是在 公元前 2000-1500 年之间的 500 年，但可能比这早几个世纪。 从这张地图上，你可以大致看到希 腊人和赫梯人是如何从他们的家 园：印欧大陆，迁徙到安纳托利亚 和巴尔干半岛的。

paragraphs.en: Anatolian and Balkan Peninsulas. The Hittites grew and prospered as a people, and by the 1500s BC they were the strongest kingdom in Anatolia. The replaced the indigenous Hattan people, and borrowed that name for themselves, calling themselves the Hittites. Here is a map of the Hittite Kingdom. Now, the Hittites are mentioned at least a dozen place in the Bible. For example in the book of Judges we can read these lines: He then went to the land of the Hittites, where he built a city and called it Luz, which is its name to this day. But for a long time, people thought the Hittites were either mythical or
paragraphs.zh: 赫梯人作为一个民族发展壮大，至 公元前 16 世纪，他们已经成为了 安纳托利亚最强的王国。他们取代 了当地的哈坦人，并沿用了他们的 名字，将自己称为赫梯人。 这是赫梯王国的版图。 赫梯人在《圣经》中出现了许多次。 例如，在《士师记》中，我们可以 读到以下段落： 然后他去了赫梯人的土地，在那里 建造了一座城市，取名为卢兹，这 个名字延用到了今天。 但在很长一段时间里，人们认为赫 梯人要么是虚构的，要么只是一个

paragraphs.en: just a minor tribe. But that way of thinking all changed in 1906 when the German archaeologist Hugo Winckler discovered the city of Hattusa in the middle ofAnatolia. Archaeologists realized that the Hittites were actually a great Kingdom and Hattusa was their capital city. Here is a picture of the ruins of Hattusa. This particular picture shows us the ruins of the main temple. At its peak, Hattusa may have had a population of 40,000 people. Like Mycenae, Hattusa was defended by strong walls, and at the entrance to one of the city gates were these carvings of two sphinxes.
paragraphs.zh: 小部落。 但是，随着考古学家雨果·温克勒 于 1906 年在安纳托利亚中部发现 了哈图萨市之后，这种看法有了改 变。 考古学家们意识到赫梯实际上是 一个伟大的王国，而哈图萨正是其 的首都。 这是哈图萨遗址的照片。这张图片 向我们展示了主圣殿的废墟。 在全盛时期，哈图萨可能有 4 万人 口。 和迈锡尼一样，哈图萨也被坚固的 城墙保护着，在城门的入口处，有 两座狮身人面像。

paragraphs.en: Now these walls probably seem short to you and there is a reason for that. The stone part of the walls was only the foundation, and that's all that remains, the upper part of the walls were probably constructed out of brick. So the actual wall in the 15th century BC was probably quite formidable. Here is a reconstruction of part of the walls of Hattusa, and it should give you a better sense of what the city looked like at the peak of its power. The Hittite language was an Indo-European language, and fortunately they also had a writing system.
paragraphs.zh: 这些墙在你看来可能不够高，但这 是有原因的。 墙的石头部分只是地基，只有这一 部分得以保存。而墙的上半部分可 能是用砖砌成的。 存于公元前 15 世纪的真正城墙体 量可能非常巨大。 这是对哈图萨部分城墙的重建，这 应该能让你更好地了解其权力巅 峰时期的样子。 赫梯语是一种印欧语，幸运的是， 他们也有文字系统。

paragraphs.en: Like most Bronze-Age civilizations in the Middle-East, the Hittites adopted the Sumerian Cuneiform system and adjusted it to fit their own language. The Hittite language was first deciphered by the Czech linguist Bedrich Hrozhny. He examined several hundred Hittite passages and thought that the word "wad-da" was probably water, and the word "Ninda" was bread, since it was that in Sumerian. From this he deciphered the phrase: "You will eat bread, also you will drink water." And then went on to decipher the rest of the language.Amazingly he did much of this work in 1917, during his spare time as a soldier while he was fighting in World War
paragraphs.zh: 如中东地区大多数青铜时代的文 明一样，赫梯人也采用了苏美尔楔 形文字系统，并对其进行了调整， 以适应他们自己的语言。 赫梯语最早是由捷克语言学家贝 德里奇·赫罗日尼破译的。 他查阅了数百篇赫梯文章，并认为 “wad-da”的意思可能是“水”， 而”Ninda”可能指“面包”。因 为这就是苏美尔语中的意思。 由此，他破译了这句话：“你会吃 面包，你也会喝水。” 随后他继续破译剩下的语言。令人 惊讶的是，他在 1917 年利用业余 时间做了很多这方面的工作，当时 他是一名士兵，正投身于一战。

paragraphs.en: One. All in all, the Hittite Kingdom lasted from around 1600 BC until 1178 BC. There were at least 24 Kings during that time and historians break this history up into 3 periods: The Old, Middle and New Kingdom. For centuries the Hittites were quite a powerful kingdom. In fact, the Hittites were so powerful, that in the 1595 BC, the Hittites were able to launch an expedition deep into Mesopotamia, defeat the Babylonian army, and capture Babylon. This defeat destroyed the old Babylonian empire, which we discussed several lessons ago. This happened under Mursili I, the 3rd Hittite King.
paragraphs.zh: 总之，赫梯王国从公元前 1600 年 持续到公元前 1178 年，期间有至 少 24 任国王。历史学家将其分为 三个阶段：古王国时期，中王国时 期和新王国时期。 几个世纪以来，赫梯一直是一个相 当强大的王国。 事实上，赫梯人如此强大，以至于 在公元前 1595 年，他们发动了一 次远征，深入美索不达米亚，击败 巴比伦军队，占领了巴比伦。 这场战争摧毁了古巴比伦王国。 这是在第三赫梯国王穆尔西里一 世的统治下发生的。

paragraphs.en: Another notable king was Muwatali II, who fought the battle of Kadesh against Egypt, the largest chariot battle in history. One of his successors was Hattusili III, who signed the peace treaty with Egypt that ended that war. This is the oldest peace treaty in history. And finally, the last Hittite King was Suppiluliuma III, who was King when the Hittite Kingdom fell, and the capital city was destroyed. The Hittites were powerful for over 400 years, and they were strong for two main reasons. First, they were Indo-Europeans, so they were masters of horsemanship. The Hittites perfected the chariot, and for many centuries it was the
paragraphs.zh: 另一位著名的国王是穆瓦塔利二 世，他曾在卡叠什战役对抗埃及， 这是历史上规模最大的车战。 他的继任者之一是哈图西里三世， 他与埃及签署了结束这场战争的 和平条约。 这是历史上最古老的和平条约。 赫梯的最后一任国王是苏庇鲁里 乌马什三世。在他任期，赫梯帝国 最终走向湮灭，而都城也遭毁灭。 赫梯人在 400 年来始终非常强大， 有两个主要原因。 首先，他们是印欧人，因此他们非 常擅长马术。赫梯人完善了战车， 许多世纪以来，它是青铜时代的超 级武器。在广阔的平原上，由战车

paragraphs.en: super-weapon of the BronzeAge world. On a big flat field, an army of chariots was unstoppable. Additionally,Anatolia has excellent supplies of metal. There is plentiful copper and tin. So the Hittites became masters of metallurgy, and produced the best bronze tools and weapons. There is also evidence that they developed early iron tools and weapons. BUT they never produced iron in more than small quantities, mainly as a luxury good. To the east of the Hittite Empire was a city-state that the Hittites called "Wilusa" Today you have probably heard of this city, we know it as "Troy". We think that Troy was never a
paragraphs.zh: 组成的军队是不可阻挡的。 另外，安纳托利亚拥有极好的金属 供应，这里有充足的铜和锡，因此， 赫梯人成为了冶金学的专家，并生 产出了最好的青铜工具和武器。 也有证据表明，他们开发了早期的 铁制工具和武器。但他们从来没有 批量生产过铁，主要是作为一种奢 侈品。 赫梯帝国东边是被他们称为“威卢 萨”的城邦。 这个城邦现在被称为“特洛伊”。 我们认为特洛伊从来都不是赫梯

paragraphs.en: direct part of the Hittite Empire, instead it was an ally that was closely tied to them. And Troy was a valuable city because it guarded the entrance to the Dardanelles straight.Any ship that wanted to sail between the Aegean and Black Sea, would need to pay Troy taxes. This made the city very valuable from a strategic perspective. Troy is also famous, because the first work of European literature is about a war between the Mycenaeans and the Trojans. Now this book was originally a poem, written by the Blind Poet Homer. The poem is called The Illiad and it is probably the single most
paragraphs.zh: 帝国的直接组成部分，相反，它是 一个与他们紧密联系在一起的朋 友。 特洛伊是一座有价值的城邦，因为 它守卫着达达尼尔海峡的入口。任 何想要在爱琴海和黑海之间航行 的船只，都需要向特洛伊缴税。 从战略的角度来看，这座城市非常 有价值。 特洛伊也很有名，因为欧洲文学的 第一部作品就是关于迈锡尼与特 洛伊之间的战争。 这本书最初是一首由盲诗人荷马 写的诗。 这首诗被称为《伊利亚特》，它可 能是希腊文学中最重要的一部作

paragraphs.en: important work of Greek Literature. The problem is that poem was written around 800 BC. But the Trojan War, if it really happened, occurred around 1200 BC. So Homer was writing about a war that occurred 400 years before he was born.And during that time, the Greeks lost the knowledge of writing. So we don't know how much of The Illiad is historical and how much is fictional. There are many things historical about The Illiad. The places mentioned in the poem are all real places.And the High-King of all the Greeks, a man named Agamemnon, is described as the King of Mycenae. Here is a map of all the different places mentioned in the Trojan war, as well as the homelands of the
paragraphs.zh: 品。 问题是，这首诗写于公元前 800 年左右。但是特洛伊战争，如果确 有其事，则大约发生在公元前 所以荷马所写的，是一场发生在他 出生以前 400 年的战争。在那段时 间里，希腊人失去了文字记载。因 此，我们并不知道《伊利亚特》中 有多少虚构的成分。 关于《伊利亚特》，有许多真实的 史实。诗文中提及的许多地名均真 实存在。所有希腊人的最高统治者 ——阿伽门农，被描述为迈锡尼的 国王。 这是一张特洛伊战争中提到的所 有不同地方的地图，以及不同角色 的故乡。

paragraphs.en: different characters. The Illiad has been read by people for almost 3000 years, so it is a very important work of literature. But for a long time, scholars believed that it was entirely fictional, and the Trojan War never happened. However, in the 1860s, 70s and 90s, two archaeologists, Frank Calvert and Heinrich Schlieman did a dig at hill near the location of Troy that is mentioned in the poem. This hill, called Hisarlik, turned out to be a "Tell" (an artificial hill created by people living in the same place for thousands of years), it contained the ruins of an ancient city! In fact, it wasn't just 1 city. The
paragraphs.zh: 《伊利亚特》已经为人传诵 3000 年，因此它是一部非常重要的文学 作品。 但在很长一段时间里，学者们认为 这完全是虚构的，而特洛伊战争从 未发生过。 然而，在 19 世纪 60 年代、70 年 代和 90 年代，两位考古学家弗兰 克·卡尔弗特和海因里希·施利曼 在这首诗中提到的特洛伊附近的 小山上挖了个洞。 这座名为“希萨利克”的山，是一 个(古代村落遗址堆积而成的)台 形土墩，即由数千年来居住在此地 的居民的人造山，里面有一整座古 老城市的废墟！ 实际上，还不止这一座城。考古学

paragraphs.en: archaeologists found 9 different cities, which spanned the years from 3000 BC to 500AD. The 7th of these cities was around at the time the Trojan war was said to have taken place. So Troy really existed, or at least there was a real city at the place Homer says there was. Perhaps the Trojan war really did happen, or at least it is based on some historical truth. Anyway, by 1400 BC, we enter the last centuries of the BronzeAge. Historians call the years from 1400-1200 BC the "Late Bronze Age" And as you can see, the entire Eastern Mediterranean was occupied by complex societies with cities,
paragraphs.zh: 家发现了 9 座城市，时间上横跨公 元前 3000-500 年。 其中第 7 座城市在特洛伊战争发 生的时候就已经存在了。 因此，特洛伊是真是存在的，至少 在荷马所说的位置，有一座真实存 在的城市。 也许特洛伊战争真的发生了，或者 至少它是基于一些历史事实。 总之，到了公元前 1400 年，我们 进入了青铜时代的最后几个世纪。 历史学家将公元前 1400-1200 年 称为“青铜时代晚期”。 如你所见，整个东地中海地区都被 复杂的社会所占据，有城邦、文字 和青铜；有迈锡尼人、赫梯人、亚

paragraphs.en: writing and bronze: The Mycenaeans, The Hittites, the Assyrians, and the Egyptians. And this lecture series is also closing in on the end of the Bronze Age, but before we finish the story of the BronzeAge Mediterranean, we need to take a step back. Because Egypt, Mesopotamia and Greece were not the ONLY places where humans had begun farming, building cities and writing. In India, China and Mexico, the same sort of changes were also beginning to happen. So before we finish learning about the BronzeAge, I want us to take a tour of the world's other BronzeAge Civilizations. Please join me next time for India!
paragraphs.zh: 述人和埃及人。 世界历史系列课程也将随着青铜 时代的终结而迎来尾声。但在结束 青铜时代的地中海的故事之前，我 们需要往后退一步。 因为埃及、美索不达米亚和希腊并 不是人类开始耕种、发明城市和文 字的唯一地方。 在印度、中国和墨西哥，同样的变 化也开始发生。 因此，在我们结束青铜时代的学习 之前，让我们了解一下该时代世界 上其他的文明。 下节课，请和我一起学习印度的故

paragraphs.en: Okay, I hope you enjoyed that lesson on the ancientAegean, the Hitties and the Trojans. If you want to learn more, I have some excellent resources to share with you. The first is a museum. The best museum in the world for the Hittites is the Museum ofAnatolian Civilization inAnkara Turkey. If you want to read something, you should of course read The Illiad for yourself. It’s one of the great works of world literature. But, if The Illiad is a little too difficult, you can also try "Black Ships Before Troy." This book was written for YoungAdult readers, so the English is easier than The Illiad, and it will tell you the full story of the Trojan war, while The Illiad will
paragraphs.zh: 事！ 希望你们喜欢关于爱琴海，赫梯人 和特洛伊人的这一课。如果你想了 解更多，我有一些很好的资源可以 和你分享。 首先是博物馆。关于赫梯文化最好 的博物馆是安托利亚文明史博物 馆，位于土耳其首都安卡拉。 如果你想读一些东西，你应该读一 读《伊利亚特》原文。它是世界上 最伟大的文学作品之一。 如果你觉得《伊利亚特》太难了， 你也可以试一试《特洛伊的黑船 （非官方译名）》。这本书是为年轻 的成人读者而写的，语言难度比 《伊利亚特》略低，而且完整地讲 述了特洛伊战争，而《伊利亚特》 仅仅叙述了部分故事。

paragraphs.en: only tell you part of the story. When it comes to other resources, The History of Ancient Greece podcast is fantastic, as is In Search of the Trojan War by Michael Wood. All right everyone，thank you very much.And I’ll see you next time.
paragraphs.zh: 至于其他资源，《古希腊历史》和 迈克尔·伍德的《为了寻找特洛伊 战争》的播客也很不错。 谢谢大家，下节课再见！

titleZh: 第十六讲：谜一样的印度河文明

paragraphs.en: Hello, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to be looking at a different part of the world: India! We will be learning about the Indus Valley Civilization, the oldest complex society in Indian History. We will be looking at a relatively long period of history, so today's lecture will cover the centuries between 3300 and 1500 BC. This is what that looks like on our timeline. As I've mentioned before, the earliest complex societies all seemed to develop in river valleys, and the Indus River Valley culture was the same. So let's begin our lesson by looking at the river which gave birth to this society.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。今天我们要到世界的另 一头——印度去看看! 我们将一起了解印度河流域文明， 这是印度历史上最古老也最复杂 的社会文明。 从时间线可以看出，这是一个相对 较长的历史时期。所以我们今天的 课程跨越了公元前 3300-1500 年， 长达十多个世纪。 正如我之前提到过的，早期的复杂 社会似乎都是从河谷地区发展而 来，印度河流域文明也不例外。因 此，我们的课程将从孕育了这个文 明的母亲河讲起。

paragraphs.en: Here is a picture of the Indus River. It's quite beautiful, right? This map on the right shows us the watershed for the Indus river.As you can see, the river begins in the Himalayan mountains and flows south-west towards the Indian Ocean. The picture on the left is a satellite photo of the river from space. The picture on the right shows us the Indus River watershed, which is mainly located in the modern country of Pakistan, but parts of it flow throughAfghanistan and India. Here are some more pictures of the Indus River. On the left is a picture of what the river looks like in the Himalayan mountains near its source. This part of a river is called the "headwaters."
paragraphs.zh: 看看这张印度河的照片，景色是不 是非常优美？ 右边这张地图就是印度河流域。我 们可以看到，这条河始于喜马拉雅 山脉，往西南方向流向印度洋。 左边的图片是印度河的卫星照片。 右边的图片是印度河流域，它主要 位于现在的巴基斯坦境内，但其中 一部分也流经阿富汗和印度。 这里还有一些印度河的图片。左边 这张是喜马拉雅山脉中印度河起 源附近的景象。这部分河流被称为 “源流”。

paragraphs.en: One the right is a picture of the river when in reaches the ocean. This part of the river is called the delta. Farming may have reached India as early as 8000 BC. There is some debate about whether the Indians developed farming on their own, or if it spread to India from the Middle East. But there is pretty strong agreement that farming was going on in the Indus River valley by at least 8000 BC. One of the earliest agricultural settlements discovered in India is the site called "Mehrgarh." Like Jarmo in Mesopotamia, Mehrgarh is the type-site for the earliest Neolithic farmers in India. There were probably many farming villages just like Mehrgarh scattered across this
paragraphs.zh: 右边是河流入海的图片。这部分河 流被称为三角洲。 早在公元前 8000 年，农业可能就 已经在印度出现了。但是关于农业 是印度人自己发展起来还是从中 东传播过来的，现在还存在一些争 议。 但可以肯定的是，早在公元前 8000 年左右，印度河流域就有人 开始农作了。 “梅赫尔格尔” 是印度最古老的 农业定居点之一。就像美索不达米 亚的贾莫一样，梅赫尔格尔是代表 印度新石器时代最早期农民的典 型地区。在当时，可能有许多像梅 赫尔格尔一样的农耕村落分布在 地球的这一端。

paragraphs.en: part of the world. Mehrgarh culture began about 7000 BC and lasted until 3300 BC, when it was replaced by the Indus Valley civilization. As you can see, Mehrgarh was not located on the river itself, but up in the foothills near the river valley. This is very similar to how Jarmo was situated. Here on these slides is a photograph of the Mehrgarh dig-site on the left, and an illustration of what a Mehrgarh house might have looked like.As you can see, the construction techniques were similar to the way houses were built in the Middle East. The oldest ceramic depictions of people in SouthAsia were discovered in Mehrgarh. This statue
paragraphs.zh: 梅赫尔格尔文化开始于公元前 7000 年，一直延续到公元前 3300 年，之后被印度河流域文明所取 代。 可以看出，梅赫尔格尔并非位于河 流之畔，而是在河谷附近的丘陵地 区。这和贾莫的位置非常相似。 这两张图片，左边是梅赫尔格尔挖 掘现场的照片，右边是一座梅赫尔 格尔房子的图示。如你所见，他们 的建筑技术与中东相似。 人们在梅赫尔格尔发现了南亚最 古老的陶制人像。从它夸张的女性 特征可以推断，这个雕像应该是象

paragraphs.en: here might be a fertility goddess because of her exaggerated female features. We have also found a copper amulet that was made around 4000 BC using the "lost-wax process", which is one of the earliest examples of that technique ever found. The SouthAsian farmers who lived in villages like Mehrgar imported the Neolithic founder crops which we've discussed several times before. To review, those were Wheat, Barley, Lentils, Peas, Chickpeas, Bitter Vetch and Flax. But these first farmers in the Indus River Valley began to domesticate NEW crops which are native to India. These include mango, muskmelon,
paragraphs.zh: 征生育的女神。 我们还发现了一个在公元前 4000 年左右用“失蜡法”制作的铜护身 符，它是这种技术最古老的代表性 作品之一。 我们之前也说过，生活在类似梅赫 尔格尔这种村落的南亚农民引进 了新石器时代的奠基农作物。这些 农作物包括： 小麦，大麦，扁豆，豌豆，鹰嘴豆， 苦野豌豆和亚麻。 同时这些印度河流域最早的一批 农民也开始培植印度原产的新作 物。 包括芒果，甜瓜，大麻，芝麻，枣

paragraphs.en: hemp, sesame, dates, and rice. And the same pattern that we've seen elsewhere repeated itself in the Indus River valley. More farming, led to more food, which led to more people, which led to more farming. And in the 5 Millennia between 8000 and 3000 BC, there was an explosion in population, and increase in settlements, and a growth of those settlements from villages into towns and cities. We have seen this pattern happen in the Near East, in Mesopotamia, on Crete, in Ukraine and it also happened here. And just like Jarmo in Mesopotamia, the people began moving away from the edges of the
paragraphs.zh: 和稻米。 我们在其他地方看到的相同模式 在印度河流域重演了。 农业的发展生产了更多的食物，养 活了更多的人口，又反过来继续促 进农业的发展。 在公元前 8000 年到公元前 3000 年之间的五千年里，人口激增，定 居点不断增加，这些定居点也不断 地从村庄发展为城镇。 我们之前看到过的发生在近东地 区，美索不达米亚，克里特岛，乌 克兰的这种模式，也同样在这里发 生。 就像美索不达米亚的贾莫一样，人 们开始从河谷的边缘地区向河流 和洪泛区移动。

paragraphs.en: river valley in towards the river and floodplain itself. By 3300 BC cities had begun to emerge along the Indus River valley, and by 2600 BC, this culture had reached its mature state. In other words, this Indus Valley Civilization developed at roughly the same time as the river valley civilizations in Egypt and Sumer. By 2600 there were hundreds of agricultural settlements and some of these had turned into proper cities. Three of the largest most important of these cities are today called Harappa, Mohenjo-Daro, and Dholavira. Sadly, these are the modern named of these places, we don't know what these people called themselves. So
paragraphs.zh: 公元前 3300 年，印度河流域开始 出现城市，到公元前 2600 年，这 种文化已经趋于成熟。 换句话说，印度河流域文明大致是 和埃及和苏美尔的流域文明在同 一时期发展的。 到公元前 2600 年，印度河流域已 经出现数百个农业定居点，其中一 些已经成为一定规模的城市。 这些城市中最大最重要的三个城 市在今天被称为哈拉帕，摩亨朱- 达罗和朵拉维那。 遗憾的是，以上都是这些地方的现 代命名，我们不知道以前的人们管 这儿叫什么。 所以我们把这个文

paragraphs.en: we call this Civilization either the Indus Valley Civilization or the Harappan Civilization. Indus Valley Civilization is interesting because they did not build huge monuments like the Sumerian Ziggurats or Egyptian Pyramids. Instead, they built amazing infrastructure for managing water. For example, at the site of Mohenjo Daro, they built an enormous bath. It was 11.88 meters long, 7 meters wide and gets as deep as 2.4 meters. To build the bath, they used finely fitted bricks and mud which was then covered with plaster and then bitumen. We are still not sure what the purpose of the Great Bath was, but
paragraphs.zh: 明称为印度河流域文明或哈拉帕 文明。 印度河流域文明很有趣，他们没有 像苏美尔金字塔或埃及金字塔那 样建造巨大的纪念碑。 相反，他们为管理水资源建立了令 人惊叹的设施。 例如，在摩亨朱-达罗，他们建造 了一个巨大的浴池。它长 11.88 米，宽 7 米，深达 2.4 米。 为了建造这个浴池，他们使用了砖 和泥，并在表面覆盖上了石膏和沥 青。 我们目前还不确定这个大浴池的 用途是什么，但许多学者认为它被

paragraphs.en: many scholars think it had a religious function related to ritual purification. But the control of water was not just limited to public baths. The houses in Mohenjo Daro all seem to have dedicated wells for drinking water and household usage. In fact there were over 700 wells in the city. These houses also had a dedicated washing place and a dedicated toilet. The toilet would then be washed out by pouring a jar of water into it. The waste would then flow through a brick pipe into a cesspit, and perhaps it would be collected later for use as fertilizer. At the time, the Indus Valley Civilization had the most sophisticated system of sanitation and water management in the world.
paragraphs.zh: 用作与宗教有关的净化仪式。 但人们对水的管理并不限于公共 浴池。摩亨朱-达罗的房屋似乎都 有专设的水井，用来提供饮用和家 用水。 事实上，该市有 700 多口 井。 这些房屋还有专门的洗衣处和专 用厕所。 厕所是通过倒入一罐水来冲洗的。 然后污物将通过砖砌管道流入粪 坑，以后也许会被收集用作肥料。 在当时，印度河流域文明拥有世界 上最先进的卫生和水资源管理系 统。

paragraphs.en: This system of water management became most sophisticated at the city of Dholavira. At that city the people built 16 large tanks around the outside of their city, which was built on an elevated platform. Unlike the other cities, Dholavira was built out of stone instead of brick.And this city was built in a desert area called the Rann of Kutch，which has no large river like the Indus. Instead there are two smaller streams called the Mansar and Manhar. Some of these reservoirs were as much as 10 meters wide, 95 meters long, and 4 meters deep, which is comparable to two Olympic Sized swimming pools. Here is a picture of one of those
paragraphs.zh: 水资源管理系统在朵拉维那城尤 为复杂。 在这里，人们在城市外围建造了 16 个大型蓄水池，这些蓄水池建 在垫高的平台上。 与其他城市不同，朵拉维那是用石 头而不是砖建造的。 这个城市建 在一个名为库奇兰恩的沙漠地区， 没有像印度河那样的大河。相反， 这里有两条溪流：曼萨尔和曼哈 尔。 其中一些蓄水池宽 10 米，长 95 米，深 4 米，大小接近两个奥运规 模的游泳池。 这是其中一个蓄水池的照片。

paragraphs.en: pools. Then they would dam the streams during the wet season and fill up the pools. There is a 13 meter slope separating the Mansar and Manhar streams, so they would take advantage of that slope by building pools in-between the two streams. They would dam the higher stream, causing it to overflow, and would then direct the overflow into their pools and finally into the lower stream. These pools would then provide them with a water reserve which could last for many months or even years. In this way the Dholavirans were able to keep their crops well watered in an environment that is almost a desert. Here is a picture of what Dholavira
paragraphs.zh: 他们会在雨季筑坝堵塞溪流来填 满水池。 这里有个 13 米的斜坡， 将曼萨尔和曼哈尔河分开，因此他 们通过在两条溪流之间建造蓄水 池对这个斜坡加以利用。 人们会堵塞高处的溪流，使其溢 流，然后将水流引导到水池中，最 后再汇入下游溪流。 这些水池可 以为他们提供可以持续数月甚至 数年的水储备。 通过这种方式，朵拉维那在近乎沙 漠的环境中仍然能够保证作物得 到充足的浇灌。 这是一张当朵拉维那的水池蓄满

paragraphs.en: might have looked like with its reservoirs full. The Indus valley civilizations also produced beautiful artifacts. The two most famous Indus-Valley Civilization artifacts are this "Priest King" soap-stone carving, and this bronze statue of a dancing girl. Perhaps as many as 40,000 people lived in some of these cities, which makes them comparable in size to the great cities of Sumer. These cities were thriving centers of agriculture, trade and industry. You should imagine bustling advanced cities, like this picture shows. Trade was very sophisticated and we have even discovered Indus Valley artifacts in Sumerian cities, so we know that the Indus Valley
paragraphs.zh: 水时的假想图。 印度河流域文明也创造了美丽的 工艺品。 印度河流域文明最有名的两件文 物就是 “祭司王”皂石雕塑和跳 舞女孩青铜雕像。 在当时，可能有多达 4 万人生活在 其中一些城市里，这使得这些城市 的规模可以与苏美尔的大城市相 媲美。 这些城市是繁荣的农业、贸易和工 业中心。你应该想象一下熙熙攘攘 的发达城市，就像这张图一样。 当时的贸易高度发达，我们甚至在 苏美尔的城市中发现了印度河流 域的工艺品，因此我们可以知道印 度河流域的人们曾经与苏美尔进

paragraphs.en: civilization people were engaged in long-distance trade with Sumer. And the Indus valley civilization ALSO developed a writing system. Here is an example of some of the symbols in their system. These symbols are seen most commonly in seals, usually there is a picture of an animal, most often it is a Zebu cow, and then 5 of six symbols directly above. We also discovered a signboard at Dholavira with 10 of these symbols, 4 of which repeat. Unfortunately, most of the Indus valley inscriptions are short, usually only 4 or 5 symbols long, and the longest is only 17 symbols long. These messages are too short, and too few to allow us to do really good
paragraphs.zh: 行长途贸易。 印度河流域文明也发展了文字系 统。下面是他们文字系统中的一些 符号。 这些符号常在印章上出现，通常是 一个动物的画像，最常见的是肩峰 牛，然后在画像上方有 5 到 6 个符 号。 我们还在朵拉维那发现了一枚标 志牌，上面有 10 个这样的符号， 其中 4 个是重复的。 遗憾的是，大多数印度河流域的铭 文都很短，通常只由 4 到 5 个符号 组成，最长的也只有 17 个符号。 这些信息太短小，因此我们无法进 行有效的数据比对，并且和楔形文

paragraphs.en: statistical comparison, and we do not have any inscriptions with both the Indus SignsAND a language that we can read like Cuneiform. So, as with Linear A, there is just not enough information for us to decode the Indus Valley script, and it remains unknown to us. Perhaps one day we will decipher the meaning, but today it remains a mystery. Because the Indus Valley script remains un-deciphered, the Indus Valley people, like the Minoans, cannot speak for themselves.As a result, there are many things that we can only guess at. We do not know the details of their religion, and what connection it had to later Indian religions. For example, this female figure might be a fertility goddess, and the
paragraphs.zh: 字不同，我们找不到印度河的符文 与目前已知的语言出现在同一幅 碑文上。 所以，就像古希腊的线性文字 A 一样，我们缺乏足够的信息去解码 印度河流域的文字，它对我们来说 是未知的。也许有一天我们会破译 出它的含义，但时至今日它仍然是 一个谜。 因为印度河流域的文字仍未被破 译，印度河流域的人们和米诺安人 一样，无法通过文字向我们讲述自 己的故事。因此，有很多事情我们 只能猜测。 我们不知道有关他们宗教的细节， 更不知道它与后来的印度宗教有 什么联系。 例如，这个女性形象可能是象征生 育的女神，而这个印章上描绘的被

paragraphs.en: horned man, surrounded by animals, depicted on this seal, might be a god. But we cannot know for sure. And then, after 1900 BC, the Indus Valley Civilization seems to have gone into decline. The exact cause of this decline is unknown, but it might be the result of climate change or an environmental crisis. Trade decreased, people buried their valuables to keep them safe, people stopped maintaining the water infrastructure, and so on. There is greater evidence of violence and disease in this period. And then, around 1700BC the civilization seems to have undergone a major collapse and many of the Indus Valley cities were
paragraphs.zh: 动物包围起来的有角的男人，可能 是一个神。但我们无法确定。 然而在公元前 1900 年以后，印度 河流域文明似乎开始了衰落。 造成衰退的确切原因尚不清楚，但 可能是气候变化或环境危机造成 的。 贸易量减少，人们埋藏自己的贵重 物品来保证安全，人们不再维护用 水设施，等等情况相继出现。 这一时期出现了大量暴力行为和 疾病肆虐的证据。 随后在公元前 1700 年左右，这个 文明似乎土崩瓦解，许多印度河流 域的城市被荒废了。

paragraphs.en: abandoned. In some places, other people came in and lived in the ruins of the cities after they had been abandoned by the original inhabitants. At that point a new group of people were beginning to move into India: the Indo-Europeans who we have learned about before. The group of Indo-Europeans who entered India are called the "Indo-Aryans" by historians, and their arrival in India marks the transition to a new age in Indian history: the VedicAge. But that is a story for another time. Now please join me next time as we shift our focus once more and look at another place where agriculture first developed: China
paragraphs.zh: 在部分区域，有外来者来到这些被 原住民废弃的城市里定居了下来。 此时，一群新的人开始进入印度： 我们之前了解过的印欧人。 进入印度的这批印欧人被历史学 家称为“印度雅利安人”，他们的 到来标志着印度历史开始迈向一 个新时代：吠陀时代。 但这又是另一个故事了。 在下次课程中，我们将会把视野转 向另一个农耕文明初露萌芽的地 方:中国。

paragraphs.en: I hope you enjoyed this lecture on the Indus Valley Civilization. Now I'd like to share some resources with you if you are interested in further study. For a detailed look at the Indus Valley Civilization, I recommend The Indus Civilization: A Contemporary Perspective by Gregory L Possehl. And for a more humorous and fun take on history, I recommend the second book in Larry Gonick's Cartoon History of the Universe. The first chapter is about the origins of Indian Civilization. The India Museum in Calcutta is an excellent museum if you want to see some of these Indus Valley artifacts for yourself. They have examples of seals with Indus Script on them.
paragraphs.zh: 我希望你们喜欢这个关于印度河 流域文明的课程。如果你有兴趣做 进一步研究的话，我想和你分享一 些资源。 要详细了解印度河流域文明，我建 议阅读格雷戈里·波塞尔的《印度 河文明：当代视角》这本书。 要想从更幽默和有趣的视角看待 历史，我推荐拉里·戈尼克的《漫 画宇宙史》。书里的第一章就是关 于印度文明的起源。 如果你想亲自看看印度河流域的 文物，加尔各答的印度博物馆是一 个很好的选择。馆藏包括了写有印 度河文字的印章。

paragraphs.en: The National Science Center in Delhi, India has some excellent displays showing daily life in the Indus Valley civilization. The National Museum of Pakistan in the city of Karachi is another great museum to go visit. The Soapstone carving of the Priest-King, perhaps the most famous Indus-Valley Civilization artifact, can be found there. And finally there is also the National Museum of India in Delhi, India. The bronze statue of the dancing girl can be seen in this museum. I actually visited this museum myself when I was travelling through India in early 2011, and I can tell you from personal experience that this museum is quite good, and they have an excellent exhibit on the
paragraphs.zh: 位于印度德里的国家科学中心展 示了印度河流域文明的日常生活。 位于卡拉奇市的巴基斯坦国家博 物馆是另一个值得参观的伟大博 物馆。在那里我们可以找到最著名 的印度河流域文明文物——祭司 王的皂石雕刻。 最后还有位于印度德里的印度国 家博物馆。 在这个博物馆里我们可以看到跳 舞女孩的铜像。我在 2011 年初的 印度之旅中亲自参观了这个博物 馆，我个人对这个博物馆的印象很 好，他们有关于印度河流域文明的 不错的展览。

paragraphs.en: Indus Valley Civilization. All right everyone,thank you very much.And I’ll see you next time.
paragraphs.zh: 这节课就到这里，谢谢大家，下节 课再见！

titleZh: 第十七讲：如繁星般绚烂的中华文明（1）

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today we are going to look at the prehistory of China. This should be a special lecture since most of you guys are Chinese. This lecture will also be a little longer than usual, since there is so much history I want to go over. We have way too much to talk about in just one lecture, so we are going to be breaking this lesson up into two parts: We are going to be looking at a very long span of history, going back at least 10,000 years. So this is what that looks like on our timeline. To the best of our knowledge, farming was discovered by humans
