paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天我们将学习中国的史前历 史。鉴于你们很多都是中国人，这 一课将非常特别。 这节课也会比平时长一点，因为有 那么多的历史我都想重温一遍。 我会将这节课分成两个部分来讲。 我们要看一段很长的历史，至少要 追溯到 10,000 年前。所以这就是 它在我们时间线上的样子。 据我们所知，在人类历史上，我们 只有过四次发明农耕的经历。

paragraphs.en: only four times in the history of our species. But it was such a powerful idea, that once it was discovered people everywhere began to adopt it, and it began to spread quickly. So far our story has focused on the discovery of farming by the Natufians in the Fertile Crescent. Then this knowledge of farming spread outwards from there. It spread northwest to Europe and southwest to Egypt.And it spread east to India. But Farming was also discovered in three other places. The second place where people discovered farming was in China, probably around 8000 BC. If you look at a satellite picture of Eurasia like this one here, you can
paragraphs.zh: 一旦有人开始农耕，全世界各地的 人们就纷纷效仿，而农耕就开始迅 速传播开来。 到目前为止，我们的故事主要集中 在富饶的新月地带，纳图夫人率先 开始耕种。然后，他们的农业知识 开始向外扩散。它向西北传播至欧 洲，西南至埃及，东至印度。 但是在其他三个地方，人们也发现 了农业。人们发现农业的第二个地 方是在中国，大概在公元前 8000 年左右。 请观察这张卫星图片，你就会明 白，为什么农业会在中国独立发

paragraphs.en: see why farming had to be invented in China, and couldn't spread there from the Middle East. The Gobi and Taklamakan deserts in the north, the Himalayan mountains in the middle and dense jungle in the south formed a natural barrier that made it difficult for people to travel from SouthAsia to East Asia. This meant that China was largely isolated from the rest of Eurasia until people developed better ships and domesticated the horse. So the Chinese developed agriculture on their own before the idea reached them from the Middle East, and then the idea of agriculture began spreading outwards from China to Korea, Japan, and SoutheastAsia. Now what crops did the ancient
paragraphs.zh: 展，而不是从中东传播过来的。 北部的戈壁和塔克拉玛干沙漠，南 部的喜马拉雅山脉和南部茂密的 丛林形成了一道天然屏障，使得人 们很难从南亚前往东亚。 这意味着中国在很大程度上与欧 亚大陆的其他地区隔绝，直到人们 发明了较为先进的船舶、驯化了马 匹。 因此，在农耕从中东传到中国之 前，中国就自己发展了农业，然后 农业观念开始从中国向外蔓延到 韩国、日本和东南亚。 那么，当中国古人发现农业的时

paragraphs.en: Chinese domesticate when they discovered farming? If you remember our previous lessons, in the Middle East, the Neolithic founder crops were wheat, barley, lentils, peas, chickpeas, vetch and flax. But these were NOT the same crops that the ancient Chinese discovered, because these plants did not naturally grow in China. Instead, Chinese agriculture was founded by a different set of plants. These crops were first Millet, then Rice, then Soybeans, then Peaches and Hemp, and finally Sago Palm. Sago Palm might seem strange, but it was actually a very important crop in the tropical parts of China early on.
paragraphs.zh: 候，他们种植了什么作物呢？ 如果你还记得我们之前的课程，在 中东，新石器时代的创始作物是小 麦、大麦、扁豆、豌豆、鹰嘴豆、 苦野豌豆和亚麻。 但这些都不是中国古代发现的作 物，因为这些植物在中国并不是自 然生长的。 相反，中国农业是由一套不同的植 物建立起来的。首先是粟，然后是 大米，然后是大豆，然后是桃子和 麻，最后是西谷椰子。 西谷椰子可能对大家来说比较陌 生，但它实际上是中国热带地区一 种非常重要的作物。

paragraphs.en: But it wasn't just the plants, the ancient Chinese also domesticated several types of animals. Like all humans, they already had domestic dogs by the end of the ice ages. But then they domesticated pigs, chickens, silk worms, ducks, geese and water buffalo. Notice that ancient China DID NOT have horses, sheep, goats or dairy cows. Instead, plants and animals that had been domesticated in Western Eurasia only reached China between 4000 and 2000 BC. This includes plants like wheat, barley, and peas, and animals like horses, goats, and sheep. And at the same time, Chinese plants and animals like ducks, geese, and chicken, as well as peaches,
paragraphs.zh: 除了植物之外，中国古人还驯化了 多种动物。 就像所有人类一样，到冰河时代 末，他们已经有了家犬，但后来他 们驯养了猪、鸡、蚕、鸭子、鹅和 水牛。 请注意，古代中国没有马、绵羊、 山羊或奶牛。 相反，在欧亚大陆西部驯化的动植 物直到公元前 4000-2000 年才到 达中国，其中包括小麦、大麦和豌 豆等植物，以及马、山羊和绵羊等 动物。 与此同时，中国的鸭、鹅、鸡，以 及桃子、麻、谷子、米等等动植物， 终于到达了欧亚大陆西部。

paragraphs.en: hemp, millet and rice, finally reached Western Eurasia. The productivity of agriculture on BOTH sides of Eurasia improved with the addition of these new domesticated plants and animals. So although China was isolated by geography, it wasn't COMPLETELY isolated, and new idea, plants, animals and diseases would always trickle in from the rest of Eurasia, just more slowly. Now, here is a question, WHY did agriculture develop in China. The answer probably has to do with rivers. As we have seen time and again, river-valleys are the mothers of civilization. The Tigris and Euphrates, the Nile, the Dnieper and the Indus, all of these are
paragraphs.zh: 随着这些驯化动植物的互通有无， 欧亚大陆两侧的农业生产力都有 所提高。 因此，尽管中国在地理上相对孤 立，但它并不是完全孤立的，新观 念、植物、动物和疾病总是从欧亚 大陆其他地方缓慢而来。 现在，这里有一个问题，为什么农 业能够在中国发展。答案可能与河 流有关。 我们一次又一次地看到，河谷是文 明之母。底格里斯河和幼发拉底 河、尼罗河、第聂伯河和印度河， 所有这些都是产生农业社会的河 谷。

paragraphs.en: river-valleys that gave birth to farming societies. For China, there are 3 major rivers, the Yellow River, the Yangtze River and the Pearl River. It was the first two, the Yellow River and Yangtze River that played the biggest role in the development of farming and civilization. Here is a map and a photograph of the Yellow River. It flows through northern China. And here is a map and a photograph of the Yangtze River, which flows through southern China.As you can see the Yangtze is the bigger river. Both rivers however, begin up in the Himalayan mountains and are fed by melting glaciers. These photographs show you the headwaters for both
paragraphs.zh: 对中国来说，有三条主要的河流， 黄河，长江和珠江。 在农业和文明的发展过程中，黄河 和长江流域发挥了最大的作用。 这是一张地图和一张黄河的照片， 流经中国北方。 这是一张地图和一张长江的照片， 流经中国南方。如你所见，长江比 黄河更大。 然而，这两条河流都是在喜马拉雅 山脉开始的，由冰川融化所滋养。 这些照片向你展示了两条河流的 源头。

paragraphs.en: rivers. In this way they are just like the Indus River, which is also fed by melting snow in the Himalayas. But, if you look at the period in Chinese history between the discovery of farming and the invention of writing, you will immediately realize something. This is an immense period of time. It covers nearly 6000 years, which is almost twice as long as China's written history. Furthermore, China is a BIG place. Today we think of it as one country, but in prehistoric times, it was large enough to have dozens of different cultures develop inside what is now modern China. I did a survey of all the different
paragraphs.zh: 通过这种方式，它们就像印度河一 样，也被喜马拉雅山脉的融雪所滋 养。 但是，如果你查看中国历史上发明 农业和发明文字中间的这段时期， 你会马上意识到一些事情。 这是一段很长的时间。它涵盖了近 6000 年的历史，几乎是中国文字 历史的两倍。 而且，中国很大。今天，我们把它 看作一个国家，但在史前时代，它 的规模足以让几十种不同的文化 在现在的现代中国发展起来。 我做了一项调查，调查了在这段时

paragraphs.en: cultures that develop during this time period, and I counted at least 28. In other words, the story of Neolithic China includes at least 28 different cultures over the span of 6000 years. That is a massive amount of material to cover. So I will not be able to go into detail in this lesson. Instead I'm only going to give you a summary of 12 of the more famous and interesting cultures and hopefully I will make you curious to learn more. Because Neolithic China deserves more study. So let's begin our story as far back as we can.As some of you may know, Hominids have lived in China for over a million years. We know this because of the Zhou Kou Dian
paragraphs.zh: 间里发展起来的所有不同的文化， 至少有 28 个。 换句话说，新石器时代的中国，在 6000年间至少发展了28种不同的 文化。 所以我不能在这节课中详细讲解 所有的文化。相反，我会总结其中 12 个有名和有趣的文化，希望我 能激发你的好奇心，去了解更多。 因为新石器时代的中国值得更多 的研究。 那么，让我们尽可能地追溯历史。 你们中有人可能知道，原始人类已 经在中国生活了 100 多万年。我们 之所以知道这一点，是因为周口店 发现了直立人化石。

paragraphs.en: fossils of Homo erectus. But as far as we know, modern Chinese people did not evolve from theAsian Homo erectus.All Homo sapiens evolved inAfrica and then we began to migrate out ofAfrica into the rest of the world about 50,000 years ago. The earliest Homo sapiens reached China about 40,000 years ago.And that is where our story begins. Now 40,000 years ago was still the middle of the last ice age. So you have to imagine small groups of Hunter-gatherers moving around the Chinese continent. Hunting animals like Mammoths and gathering edible plants. Eventually around 10,000 years ago, the ice-ages ended, and the Earth warmed up.As the climate changed
paragraphs.zh: 但据我们所知，现代中国人并不是 从亚洲直立人进化而来的。所有的 智人都是在非洲进化的。5 万年 前，人类开始从非洲移居到世界其 他地方。 最早的智人在四万年前到达中国， 这也是我们故事的开始。 4 万年前仍然是最后一个冰河时 代的中期。所以你得这么想：一小 群狩猎采集者在中国大陆四处迁 移。捕猎猛犸象之类的动物、收集 可食用植物。 最终，大约一万年前，冰河时代结 束了，地球变暖了。随着中国气候 的变化，人们开始以新的方式生

paragraphs.en: in China, people began living in new ways. One of the oldest stone-age sites in China is Xian Ren Cave, which is located in modern Jiangxi province, in Da Yuan township. The cave was probably occupied all the way back in the last ice-age, as long ago as 16,000 BC. But the cave was such a good natural shelter, that people probably continued to inhabit it for thousands of years, all the way into the Neolithic period. In this way it’s very similar to the Franchthi cave in Greece. In Xianren Cave we find evidence of very ancient pottery (perhaps the oldest in the world) and what might be rice cultivation (although it might also be just wild rice.)
paragraphs.zh: 活。 中国最古老的石器时代遗址之一 是坐落于江西大源的仙人洞。 早在公元前 16,000 年，最后一个 冰河时代，就已经有人常住在这个 洞穴里了。 这个洞穴是一个很好的自然避难 所，因此人们可能会在此居住数千 年，一直到新石器时代。从这一点 上来看，它和希腊的弗兰克西洞穴 非常相似。 在仙人洞里，我们发现了非常古老 的陶器（也许是世界上最古老的陶 器）和可能是水稻栽培的证据（尽 管可能也只是野生水稻）。

paragraphs.en: The next important archaeological site is Nan Zhuang Tou, which is located in Hebei near Xushui county. This site was discovered under a peat bog, which makes sense because the low-oxygen environment of peat bogs makes them very good at preserving artifacts. Here in Nan Zhuang Tou we can find the earliest solid evidence of plant domestication. We think around 8500 BC, the people living here began to cultivate millet. We have also found stone grinding slabs and rollers that show us they were grinding the millet into flour. There is also evidence that people at Nan Zhuang Tou had domesticated dogs.
paragraphs.zh: 下一个重要的考古遗址是南庄头， 它位于河北附近，靠近徐水县。 这个地点是在泥炭沼泽下发现的， 这是有道理的，因为泥炭沼泽的低 氧环境非常适合保存史前古器物。 在南庄头，我们可以找到最早的植 物驯化的确凿证据。我们认为大约 在公元前 8500 年，这里的人们开 始种植粟。我们还发现了石磨板和 滚轴。这些文物告诉我们，他们会 把粟磨成粉。 也有证据表明，南庄头的人已经驯 养了狗。

paragraphs.en: The details of how this process happened are not understood as well as they are for the Natufians in the Middle East, but I imagine the process was pretty similar. In my mind, the people of Nanzhuangtou are like the Chinese Natufians. The next culture I'd like to mention is the Pei Li Gang culture in the Yi-Luo river basin in modern Henan province. The Pei li gang culture began around 7000 BC, so it is over a thousand years younger than the Nan Zhuang Tou culture. To put that in perspective, the amount of time in-between the Pei Li Gang culture and Nan Zhuang Tou culture is similar to the time between us today and the Song dynasty!
paragraphs.zh: 这一过程的细节还不清楚，就像中 东的纳图夫人一样，但我认为这个 过程是非常相似的。在我看来，南 庄头人就像中国的纳图夫人。 我想提到的下一种文化是在现代 河南省的伊洛河流域的裴李岗文 化。 裴李岗文化起源于公元前 7000 年 左右，比南庄头文化年轻一千多 年。 从这个角度来看，裴李岗文化和南 庄头文化之间的时间差，相当于我 们今天和宋朝！

paragraphs.en: By the time of the Pei Li Gang culture, agriculture had become more developed. Millet was still the staple crop, but people had also begun raising pigs, cattle and chicken. Hunting was still important; people hunted wild deer, wild boar and fished for carp. And people were growing hemp to use for making fibers, probably for clothes and for rope. They probably made fishing nets out of hemp. By this time, farming had allowed the population to grow, because there were probably around 100 villages connected by the Pei Li Gang culture, all living in an area of around 100 square kilometers. Pei Li Gang culture is also one of
paragraphs.zh: 到了裴李岗文化时，农业更为发达 了。 粟仍然是主要农作物，但人们也开 始饲养猪、牛和鸡。 狩猎还是很重要。人们猎杀野鹿、 野猪，钓鲤鱼。 人们种植麻类植物来编织纤维，可 能用于制作服饰和绳子，他们可能 还用麻来制作渔网。 到那时，农业的发展已经允许人口 增长，因为大约有 100 个村庄被裴 李岗文化连接起来，所有的村庄都 生活在大约 100 平方公里的范围 内。 裴李岗文化也是中国最早的陶器

paragraphs.en: the first Chinese cultures to make pottery (although there was some pottery and Xianren Cave if you remember), and they also seem to have had dedicated places for burying their dead. Their tools were still all made out of stone and archaeologists have found stone arrowheads, spear heads, chisels, axes, and sickles. Several days of travel to the southwest of the Pei Li Gang culture, there was another settlement, which we now call Jiahu. Archaeologists argue about whether Jiahu was part of Pei li Gang culture or separate from it. It’s close enough to be connected, but still relatively isolated from the other Pei Li Gang settlements. But there are some key differences.
paragraphs.zh: 文化之一，而且他们似乎也有专门 的埋葬死者的地方。 他们的工具仍然是用石头做的，考 古学家已经发现了石箭头、矛头、 凿子、斧头和镰刀。 从裴李岗文化遗址向西南方向走 几天，就又到了另一个遗址：贾湖 遗址。 考古学家们曾经争论过，贾湖究竟 是裴李岗文化的一部分，还是与之 分离。它的距离非常近，但仍然相 对隔绝于其他裴李岗定居点。 但还有几个关键区别：贾湖比其他

paragraphs.en: Jiahu was several hundred years older that the other Pei Li Gang settlements, (although later Jiahu existed at the same time as Pei Li Gang.) And the Jiahu settlement grew rice, which the Pei Li Gang settlements did NOT do. At Jiahu, archaeologists have found several flutes, made from crane bones and several interesting turtle shells, more on those in a moment. Jiahu isALSO the earliest evidence of alcohol, which was made out of rice, honey, grapes and hawthorn. We think the community at Jiahu survived until 5700 BC, when it was destroyed by a flood. By comparison, Jiahu is about as old as Cataolhoyuk in Turkey, orAin
paragraphs.zh: 裴李岗定居点早了数百年。（尽管 后来也同期存在过） 而且，贾湖人种水稻，而裴李岗定 居点则没有。 在贾湖遗址，考古学家发现了用鹤 骨制成的长笛，以及数个有趣的龟 壳。稍后再详细介绍。 贾湖遗址还出土了最早证据，证明 当时已有酒精，是由大米、蜂蜜、 葡萄和山楂制成的。 我们认为贾湖直到公元前 5700 年 才被洪水摧毁。 比较而言，贾湖和土耳其恰塔霍裕 克遗址、约旦的艾因加扎勒一样古

paragraphs.en: Ghazal in Jordan. At Jiahu, some of the bodies were buried with various objects, like pottery, tools or tortoise shells. Most controversially, some of those tortoise shells were carved with symbols. There is a lot of debate over those symbols. Are they just pictures, or do they represent the earliest examples of Chinese characters? The Jiahu symbols are 4000 years older than the Oracle bones, so if they are actual writing, then they are the oldest writing in the world. BUT, that would be a pretty radical claim to make, and most scholars are too cautious to make it. There's just not enough evidence. Also, you should know that
paragraphs.zh: 老。 在贾湖遗址，很多尸体都有各种物 体陪葬，例如陶瓷、工具或龟壳。 最具争议的是，一些龟壳上刻着一 些符号。关于这些符号有很多争 论。 它们只是图案，还是代表了最早的 汉字？ 贾湖遗址的这些符号比甲骨文还 要早了 4000 年，因此如果这些是 文字，那么它们就是世界上最古老 的文字。 不过，这是一个相当激进的主张， 很多学者都非常谨慎，无法证明这 一点。目前还没有足够的证据。 而且，你应该知道，在当时，类似

paragraphs.en: symbolic pictures appear all across the world at this time in history, not just at Jiahu. For example, the Jiahu symbols are from around 6000 BC. We have also found other examples of this sort of writing on the other side of Eurasia in Romania and Serbia. This writing is called the Vinca symbols, and it dates to 5300 BC. As with the Jiahu symbols in China, many people want to believe that the Vinca symbols are writing. But others think that they are just a form of proto-writing: symbols but not yet a full language. The problem with both the Jiahu symbols and the Vinca symbols is that we just don’t have enough evidence. The next culture that I want to
paragraphs.zh: 的象征性图画出现在全世界各个 角落，并不仅仅只在贾湖。 例如，贾湖的符号大约是在公元前 6000 年左右。我们还在欧亚大陆 另一边的罗马尼亚和塞尔维亚也 发现了类似的例子。这种文字被称 为温卡符号，它的历史可以追溯到 公元前 5300 年。 就和中国的贾湖符号一样，许多人 认为温卡符号也是文字。但也有人 认为，这只是一种类文字：是符号， 但还不是完整的语言。 贾湖符号和温卡符号的问题在于： 证据不足。 我想提到的下一种文化是在浙江

paragraphs.en: mention is Kua Hu Qiao, near modern Hangzhou, in Zhejiang province. This community began about 6000 BC, which means it began 1000 yearsAFTER Jiahu began, BUT early Kua hu qiao and late Jiahu overlapped for 300 years. The people at Kua Hu Qiao were already advanced rice farmers and made very sophisticated pottery. Archaeologists have also discovered an ancient dug-out canoe, and the world's oldest domesticated peach, here at Kua Hu Qiao. Around the same time (5500 BC) we ALSO have the He Mu Du culture, which thrived just south of Hangzhou bay, right next to the ocean.
paragraphs.zh: 杭州附近的跨湖桥遗址。 这个群落始于公元前 6000 年左 右，这意味着它是贾湖兴起后 1000 年才开始的，但早期的跨湖 桥和晚期的贾湖相交却有 300 年 之久。 跨湖桥人已经是高级稻农了，他们 制作了非常复杂的陶器。 考古学家还在跨湖桥遗址发现了 一种古老的独木舟，以及世界上最 古老的人工种植桃。 大约在同一时间(公元前 5500 年)，杭州湾以南的海边还出现了 河姆渡文化。

paragraphs.en: The He Mu Du people are interesting because some scholars think they were a separate ethnic group from modern Chinese people. Some scholars think that the He Mu Du people might be ancestors to the Austronesian cultures which later settled Taiwan and then travelled across the Pacific Ocean, going as far as Hawaii and New Zealand. Because the He Mu Du people liked to live in wet marshy areas that were prone to frequent flooding, they put their houses on stilts to keep them above the water during floods. We also think they lived in communal long-houses, just like the Linear Pottery people who lived in Europe at the same time. You can still find stilt houses like this in modern Malaysia.
paragraphs.zh: 河姆渡人之所以有趣，是因为学者 认为，他们是一个与现代中国人是 同种同文化的独立民族。 一些学者认为，河姆渡人可能是南 岛文化的祖先，后来定居台湾，然 后穿越太平洋，远至夏威夷和新西 兰。 由于河姆渡人喜欢住在潮湿的沼 泽地区，那里经常发生洪水，他们 将房屋建在高跷上，以保证汛期时 房屋不会被水淹没。 我们认为他们也住在公共的长屋 中，就和欧洲线纹陶文化的人一 样。在现代马来西亚，你仍然可以 找到像这样的棚屋。

paragraphs.en: This is a picture of a model that should give you an idea of what a He Mu Du village looked like. There is linguistic evidence and some archaeological evidence that theAustronesian people who settled the Pacific Ocean between 3000 BC and 1000AD were originally from Taiwan. This is called the "Out of Taiwan Hypothesis." And before they lived in Taiwan, they may have lived in He Mu du. Perhaps the He Mu Du culture is the ancestor culture to people who live as far away as New Zealand, Hawaii, and Madagascar. Here is picture of a modern Austronesian person, and here are the famous heads on Easter Island, which was also an Austronesian culture.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一张模型图，从中你可以大致 了解河姆渡人的村庄是什么样的。 有语言学和一些考古证据表明，于 公元前3000年到公元1000年之间 定居太平洋的南岛人最初来自台 湾。这被称为“走出台湾假说”。 在他们定居台湾之前，他们可能曾 在河姆渡居住。或许河姆渡文化正 是新西兰、夏威夷和马达加斯加文 化的祖先。 这是一张现代南岛人的照片，这些 是复活节岛上那些著名的巨石头 像，这也是一种南岛文化。

paragraphs.en: If the Out-of-Taiwan theory is correct, these people are descended from the inhabitants of He Mu Du. Next we have the Yangshao culture, whose type-site, Banpo village, is probably the most famous Neolithic community in China. As far as we can tell, the Yangshao culture is the successor to the Pei Li Gang culture, and the archaeological sites are located in Shaanxi province. The Yangshao people practiced slash and burn agriculture. They would burn down the trees in an area, farm the land until it was exhausted, and then move on to a new location. Or at least that was the practice early in their history. Their staple crops were millet and rice, and they may have practiced
paragraphs.zh: 如果“走出 台湾假说”成立，那 么这些人就是河姆渡居民的后裔。 接下来我要讲的是仰韶文化，它的 模式遗迹——半坡村，应该是中国 最有名的新石器时代遗址。 据我们所知，仰韶文化是裴李岗文 化的继任，考古遗址位于陕西省。 仰韶人采用刀耕火种法，他们会将 一个地区的树都烧毁，在此耕种， 直到资源被耗尽。随后他们就会转 移到下一个地区。或者至少这是他 们历史上的早期实践。 他们的主要农作物是粟和水稻，并 且，他们可能已经进行了非常早期

paragraphs.en: very early silkworm cultivation. But, hemp was still the main fabric for clothing, silk would have been an extreme luxury. Pottery was already quite sophisticated by the Yangshao period. We have some idea about how the Yangshao people dressed. Men wore loin-cloths and tied their hair in a top-knot. Women wrapped cloths around themselves and tied their hair up in a bun. Yangshao houses were circular in shape, and constructed by digging a pit and then poles would support the roof. The roof would be made of thatched millet straw and the walls were made out of wattle-and-daub
paragraphs.zh: 的桑蚕栽培。 但是，麻仍然是服装的主要面料， 丝绸只是一种极端的奢侈品。 仰韶时期，陶器已经相当复杂了。 我们对仰韶人的穿着有了一些了 解。男人和穿束腰布，把头发扎成 一个顶髻。 女人把衣服裹在身上，把头发扎成 一个小圆髻。 仰韶的房子呈圆形，通过挖坑而 建，用杆子支撑屋顶。屋顶是用覆 盖了茅草的的谷草，墙壁也使用了 抹灰篱笆墙结构，并使用泥土作为 主要的填充材料。

paragraphs.en: construction, with mud used as the main filler material. Avillage might cover several acres, and there was usually a central square in the middle. Yangshao culture lasted from 5000 to 3000 BC. So this culture was ending just as Sumer and Egypt were beginning. At the Yangshao village of Jiangzhai, archaeologists have found the earliest copper artifacts in China. Yangshao culture marks the end of the Chinese stone age and the beginning of the copper age. As with Jiahu, archaeologist have found symbols engraved onto pottery shards. There is also debate over whether these Banpo symbols are just
paragraphs.zh: 一个村庄可能占地几英亩，中间通 常会有一个中央广场。 仰韶文化从公元前 5000 年持续到 公元前 3000 年。也就是说，当苏 美尔和埃及开始兴起的时候，这种 文化就结束了。 在姜斋的仰韶村，考古学家发现了 中国最早的铜制品。仰韶文化标志 着中国石器时代的结束和铜器时 代的开始。 和贾湖一样，考古学家在陶器碎片 上发现了一些符号。 关于这些半坡陶符究竟只是图片、 装饰，还是一种类文字，至今仍存

paragraphs.en: pictures, decoration, or a form of proto-writing. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lecture on ancient China. Remember this is only part 1, we will look at part 2 next time, and get all the way to the Xia and Shang dynasties and the beginning of traditional Chinese history. If you are curious to learn more, here are two interesting museums you could go see. The first is the Shanxi Museum in Taiyuan. I've actually visited this museum myself in 2011 when I lived in Shanxi province. They have some good artifacts from the Neolithic period in China, and of course lots of good stuff from later Chinese history as well. If you live in Shanxi province, or ever go
paragraphs.zh: 在争议。 好了，希望你们都喜欢这节课。别 忘了，这只是中国历史的第一部 分。下节课，我们将一直讲到夏朝 和商朝。 如果你想了解更多，有两个不错的 博物馆，你可以去看看。 第一个是太原的山西博物馆。2011 年，我在山西的时候参观了这个博 物馆。 其中有一些来自中国新石器时代 的优秀文物，当然还有中国历史上 的很多好东西。如果你住在山西 省，或者去过太原，我想博物馆是 这个城市最好的去处之一。

paragraphs.en: through Taiyuan, I think the museum is one of the best things to see in that city. The second site is the Banpo Neolithic village in Xian. I haven't been there myself, but I remember seeing signs for it when I visited Xian years ago. My colleage Natalie has been there and she said it’s much more interesting if you study-up on the Neolithic first, that way you understand the context for it much better and that makes it more meaningful to go see.
paragraphs.zh: 第二个是西安的半坡新石器时代 遗址村。这一处我还没有去过，但 我记得几年前，我去西安的时候， 曾经看到过一些标志。 我的同事 Natalie 曾经去过那里， 她说，如果你能提前了解一些新石 器时代的知识，再去参观，就能够 让这趟行程更有意义。

titleEn: The Origins of Chinese Civilization II
titleZh: 第十八讲：如繁星般绚烂的中华文明（2）

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today we are going to look at part 2 of the prehistory of China. Here again is what this period in Chinese history looks like on our big timeline. To the east of the Yangshao culture was another society which archaeologists call the Da Wen Kou culture. This culture lasted from 4100 to 2600 BC, it was mainly located in what is now modern Shandong province. As with the He Mu Du people, the Da Wen Kou people may have been Austronesian instead of Han. But this point is still much debated.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天我们将继续讨论中国历 史。 这是中国历史上的这段时间在我 们大时间线上的样子。 在仰韶文化的东面，是另一个被考 古学家称为“大汶口文化”的社会 遗址。 这种文化从公元前 4100 年一直持 续到公元前 2600 年，主要分布在 现在的山东省。 与河姆渡人一样，大汶口人可能并 不是汉人，而是南岛人。但这一点 依然存疑。

paragraphs.en: The Da Wen Kou people buried their dead with grave-goods.As their culture progressed there become strong differences in the amount and quality of grave goods that individuals were buried with. This is evidence that society was become less equal and more hierarchical at this time. The Liangzhu culture existed from 3400 to 2250 BC, and like the Da Wen Kou and He Mu Du people, they may have beenAustronesian in ethnicity instead of Han. Their archaeological sites can be found in modern Zhejiang province. In any case, they lived in the Yangtze river delta, and were a highly stratified society: there was a large difference between the rich and the poor at this point.
paragraphs.zh: 大汶口人把尸体埋在坟墓里。随着 文化的发展，个人陪葬品在数量和 质量上逐渐有了很大的差异。 这证明了当时社会已经不那么公 平，出现了等级制度。 良 渚 文 化 存 在 于 公 元 前 3400-2250 年，就像大汶口和河姆 渡人一样，他们也可能是南岛人， 而不是汉族。他们的考古遗址位于 浙江省。 总之，他们居住在长江三角洲流 域，是一个高度分层的社会：富人 和穷人之间有着巨大的差距。

paragraphs.en: They made beautiful works of art out of jade, and their pottery was quite advanced. But what is more, we now know that they built large cities. In 2006 we discovered a settlement with large walls, sophisticated dams and water control, which may have had a population as high as 25,000 people. This city existed in the Yangtze river delta around 3000 BC. This LiangzhuAncient City would have been comparable in size and age to the great cities of Sumer, like Uruk. Up north, in the Yellow River Valley, in modern Gansu province was the Ma Jia Yao culture, which we think was the successor culture to the Yangshao. This culture lasted from 3300 to 2000 BC.
paragraphs.zh: 他们用玉做除了优美的艺术品，他 们的陶器也很先进。 但更重要的是，他们建造了城市。 2006 年，我们发现了一处有大墙、 复杂的水坝和水资源管理系统的 定居点，那里的人口可能高达 这个城市在公元前 3000 年左右存 在于长江三角洲。 这个良渚古城在大小和时代上都 可以与苏美尔的大城市媲美，比如 乌鲁克。 在北方的黄河流域，位于现代甘肃 省的是马家瑶文化，我们认为这是 仰韶文化的继承者。这种文化从公 元前 3300 年持续到公元前 2000 年。

paragraphs.en: The Ma Jia Yao had very sophisticated pottery, and by this point ceramics were being produced by professionals at large, centralized workshops. This is more evidence of a society that was becoming increasingly complex. But perhaps most important of all, a bronze knife was discovered at a Ma Jia Yao site near Dongxiang, Gansu. The knife was made around 2800 BC. This is the earliest bronze object that we have ever found in China. This means that the BronzeAge had begun. As we were researching this lesson, my colleague Natalie and I discovered that Baidu Baike suffers from a serious oversight in its information about the Ma Jia Yao
paragraphs.zh: 马家瑶人制造了非常精密的陶器， 而且是由专业人士在大型的集中 车间生产的。这证明了当时的社会 已经日渐复杂。 但最重要的是，在甘肃东乡附近的 马家瑶遗址发现了一把青铜刀。这 把刀是在公元前 2800 年左右制造 的。这是我们在中国发现的最早的 青铜制品。 这意味着青铜时代开始了。 当我们教研这一课的时候，我和同 事 Natalie 发现，百度百科中关于 马家瑶文化的信息有严重的疏漏。

paragraphs.en: culture. Wikipedia includes information about that bronze knife, but Baidu Baike does not, this seems like a major omission, since this knife is THE artifact that indicates the end of the CopperAge and the beginning of the Bronze age in China. Returning to our overview of the cultures; next, we have the Long Shan culture in what is now modern Shandong. The first discovery of this culture happened at a site called "Cheng Zi Ya" in 1930. The Long Shan culture lasted from around 3000 to 1900 BC. The 3rd Millennium BC saw a population explosion across China, and a dramatic increase in the size
paragraphs.zh: 维基百科包含了关于青铜刀的信 息，但百度百科没有，这是一个重 大的疏漏，因为这把刀是标志着中 国铜时代结束和青铜时代开始的 产物。 回到我们的课程概述；接下来，我 们将讲述位于现代山东的龙山文 化。 第一次发现这个文化遗址，是在 1930 年的城子崖。 龙 山 文 化 存 在 于 约 公 元 前 公元前 3000-2000 年间，中国人口 发生了爆炸式增长，定居点的规模 和数量也大幅增加。

paragraphs.en: and number of settlements. Cheng Zi Ya was a proper city with large rammed-earth walls and a population in the tens of thousands. By the time of the Long Shan culture, China had become a fully urbanized part of the world. The Longshan people also made exquisite pottery, called "black eggshell pottery" because of its color and thinness. By the 3rd Millenium BC, trade had brought many crops all the way from the fertile crescent, so the Chinese began planting wheat for the first time. As with Banpo and Jiahu, there is some evidence for proto-writing at the Longshan settlement of Dinggong.
paragraphs.zh: 城子崖是一个名副其实的城市，有 巨大的夯土城墙和成千上万的人 口。到龙山文化的时代，中国已经 率先实现了完全城市化。 龙山人还制作了精美的陶器，因其 黑色的外形和薄薄的质感而被称 为“蛋壳黑陶”。 到了公元前第三个世纪，新月沃土 的许多农作物通过贸易来到中国， 因此中国人第一次开始种植小麦。 与半坡和贾湖一样，丁公遗址的龙 山文化也出土了一些类文字。

paragraphs.en: Longshan culture appears to have been strongest in Shandong, and in the center of the yellow river. Eventually this central Yellow-River part of Longshan culture would evolve into the Er li Tou culture around 1900 BC. And now we reach the end of our journey, because Chinese archeologists think that THIS Erlitou culture, was the 夏 dynasty. Which as you all know, is the beginning of traditional Chinese history. This has not been proven, since true writing had not yet been invented in China. (Or at least we haven't found any so far). Therefore, we have no written records to confirm the Erlitou-Xia connection. But the Er Li Tou culture existed in
paragraphs.zh: 龙山文化可能是山东、黄河中段最 强盛的文化。 最终，位于黄河中段的龙山文化在 公元前 1900 年左右演变为二里头 文化。 现在，我们抵达了旅程的终点。因 为中国考古学家认为，二里头文化 就是大家熟知的夏朝。而夏朝就是 传统中国历史的开端。 这一点还没有得到证实，因为在当 时的中国，真正的文字还没有发 明。（或者至少到目前为止，我们 还没有发现任何相关证明。）因此， 我们还没有书面记录来证明二里 头文化和夏朝的关系。 但是，二里头文化与夏朝确实存在

paragraphs.en: the same time and the same place as the Xia Dynasty. So if the Xia was a real culture, it was this one. The undisputed invention of writing did not happen until the end of the 夏 dynasty and the middle of the 商 dynasty. Because it is at that point that we have the first unambiguous writing in China: The Oracle Bones. And so it is not until 1250 BC, nearly 7000 years after the discovery of farming, that proper Chinese history begins. Here at long last we can leave the realm of archaeology and legends and begin true history. It is sometimes said that China has 5000 years of history. But now that we've done this review, I think you can see why that statement is not really accurate. China has 3200 years of written
paragraphs.zh: 于同一时期和同一地区。因此，如 果夏确实是一种文化，那就是二里 头文化。 文字的确实证据，一直到夏朝结 束、商朝中期才出现。到这个时候， 中国首次出现了确切的文字：甲骨 文。 因此，直到公元前 1250 年，在发 现农业的近 7000 年后，中国的历 史才刚刚开始。在这里，我们终于 可以离开考古学和传说的领域，开 始真正的历史了。 有人说，中国有 5000 多年的历史。 但是现在我们做了回顾之后，我想 你们可以理解为什么这个表述是 不准确的。 中国有文字记载的历史有 3200

paragraphs.en: history, about 5000 years of legends, and nearly 9000 years of agriculture. China has 3200 years of written history, about 5000 years of legends, and nearly 9000 years of agriculture. I've put all of these cultures together on a timeline that goes from 7000 BC to 1AD. On the right side of the timeline you can see the dynasties which have a historical record, the Shang, the Zhou, the Qin and the Han. Proper Chinese history requires writing, and that began during the Shang. BUT there are legends that go back further. If you include the Xia Dynasty and the 3 sovereigns and 5 emperors, you could maybe push things back to 2800 BC. But while the Xia Dynasty might be based on Erlitou culture, the 3 sovereigns and
paragraphs.zh: 年，还有 5000 年的传说和 9000 年的农业。 中国有文字记载的历史有 3200 年，还有 5000 年的传说和 9000 年的农业。 我把所有这些文化都放在一个从 公元前 7000 年到公元 1 年的时间 线上。在时间线的右侧，你可以看 到有历史记录的朝代：商，周，秦 和汉。正确的中国历史需要文字记 载，而这是从商朝开始的。 但有一些传说可以追溯到更久之 前。如果包含夏朝、三皇五帝在内， 你可以把事情推回到公元前 2800 年。但是，尽管夏朝可能以二里头 文化为基础，但三皇五帝都是神话 人物，就和希腊神话中的普罗米修 斯一样。我们应该学习这些故事，

paragraphs.en: 5 emperors are mythical figures, like the Greek story of Prometheus. We should learn the stories, because they are important to Chinese culture, but we should be cautious about calling them actual "history" as opposed to "legends." What we do have is a very long and rich archaeological record of settled agriculture, and that goes back MUCH farther than the legends. The archaeological record takes us back to at least 7000 BC. And even further if you include Nan Zhuang Tou or Xianren Cave. But that's a much more complicated story, it requires work to understand, and doesn't make for a nice slogan like saying "5000 years of history!" But those 9000 years are rich and fascinating, and I hope you want to
paragraphs.zh: 因为它们对中国文化很重要，但我 们应该保持谨慎，不能称它们为实 际的“历史”，而是“传说”。 我们目前拥有的是一份很长、很丰 富的关于定居农业的考古记录，比 传说还要久远得多。考古记录至少 把我们带回了公元前 7000 年。如 果将南庄头或仙人洞包括在内，那 就更远了。 但那将会是更多更复杂的故事，需 要我们花更多的时间和精力去探 寻，而不是简单地喊出漂亮的口 号：“上下五千年”！ 这 9000 年的中国历史丰富而迷 人，希望你们能够更深入地去了

paragraphs.en: learn more about them! I certainly do! Now in the early 20th century, a group of Chinese scholars became skeptical of the traditional historical narrative of The San Huang Wu Di, followed by the Xia Dynasty, followed by the Shang dynasty, followed by the Zhou Dynasty. These men pointed out that we did not have any actual records or artifacts from anything earlier than the Zhou dynasty.And they said that we should be more skeptical about the traditional story for anything earlier than the Zhou dynasty. These thinking became known as the DoubtingAntiquity school. And they had a point. You see most of our knowledge of the Shang and Xia dynasties comes from the Great
paragraphs.zh: 解。 20 世纪初，一群中国学者开始对 三皇五帝的传统历史故事持怀疑 态度，其次是夏朝，商朝，和周朝。 他们指出，我们没有任何比周朝更 早的真实记录或文物。他们说，我 们应该对周朝以前的任何传统故 事持怀疑态度。 这种观点被称为古史辨派。 他们的观点是有一定道理的。你 看，我们对商朝和夏朝的大部分认 知都来自伟大的历史学家司马迁

paragraphs.en: Historian Sima Qian and his great work, the "Shi Ji". But Sima Qian lived during the early Han Dynasty. So he was born 900 yearsAFTER the end of the Shang dynasty and 1500 yearsAFTER the Xia Dynasty. The doubting antiquity school wondered how Sima Qian could possible have known all of these details about Chinese history which happened that long before his life. However, our understanding of the Shang began to change in the early 20th century. In 1899, two Chinese scholar-officials, Wang Yirong and Liu E discovered that some bones, which were being ground up into medicine in Henan province, actually had ancient writing
paragraphs.zh: 和他的伟大著作《史记》。 但司马迁生活在汉初。也就是说， 他出生在商朝灭亡后的 900 年，在 夏朝之后的 1500 年。 古史辨派想知道司马迁是如何得 知这些中国历史的细节的——毕 竟这些细节在他出生前就已经发 生了。 然而，我们对商朝的理解在 20 世 纪初开始发生变化。 1899 年，两名中国学者——王懿 荣和刘鹗在河南发现了一些被碾 碎入药的骨头，上面刻着文字。

paragraphs.en: inscribed on them. This discovery of the Oracle bones suddenly gave us our first written records of Chinese history from before the Zhou dynasty. However, at that point, China was in chaos, so for almost 20 years, it was difficult for real scholars to collect and study the bones, and an unknown number were looted or destroyed before they could be properly studied. Fortunately, beginning in 1917, real scholars, archaeologists and historians were finally able to begin proper research into the oracle bones. Four of the most important of these scholars were: Luo Zhenyu, Fu Sinian, James Menzies, and Li Ji. From 1917 until 1937, they were
paragraphs.zh: 甲骨文的发现，让我们一下子有了 从周朝开始的文字记录。 然而，在这个时候，中国陷入了战 乱。所以将近 20 年间，学者很难 收集和研究这些骨头。在他们真正 开始研究之前，有数不清的骨头被 掠夺和摧毁。 幸运的是，从 1917 年开始，真正 的学者、考古学家和历史学家终于 能够开始对甲骨文进行适当的研 究。 这些学者中最重要的四位是：罗振 玉、傅斯年、明义士和李濟。从 1917 年到 1937 年，他们能够为了 历史而研究和记录甲骨文。

paragraphs.en: able to study and document the oracle bones for the benefit of history. Of course, this work was interrupted in 1937 by the Japanese invasion, but it continued after 1945. And one of the big discoveries made by the oracle bones, was that the bones confirmed Sima Qian's genealogy of the Shang Kings. Sima Qian got the names and order of the different Shang Kings right. He did make a few mistakes, especially for the earliest kings, but his list was largely correct. This independent confirmation of Sima Qian's work by the Oracle bones, strengthened his credibility as a source. So if Sima Qian was right about the
paragraphs.zh: 当然，由于日本入侵，这项工作在 1937 年中断，但在 1945 年后得以 继续。 其中一项重要发现是，这些甲骨文 证实了司马迁所著的商王家谱。 司马迁获取了商朝各君王的名称 和在位顺序。 他确实犯了一些错误，但是对于中 国最早期君王的信息，他的名单在 很大程度上是正确的。 这是甲骨文对《史记》信息的独立 证明，证实了《史记》内容的准确 性。 所以，如果司马迁对商朝的观点是

paragraphs.en: Shang Dynasty, maybe he was also right about the Xia dynasty. For this reason, in 1993, the Chinese scholar Li Xueqin gave an influential speech where he called upon Chinese historians to accept the traditional narrative written by Sima Qian, and leave the Doubting Antiquity Movement. He said we should instead be part of a "BelievingAntiquity" movement. But this debate about whether or not we could trust Sima Qian was healthy. Because it is through this king of critical thinking and questioning, that we can deepen our understanding of history. I believe that the point of historical study is NOT to promote nationalism, or feel good about the past. The point is to find the truth
paragraphs.zh: 正确的，也许他对夏朝的看法也是 正确的。 为此，1993 年，中国学者李学勤 发表了一篇颇具影响力的演讲，他 呼吁中国历史学家抛弃古史辨派， 接受司马迁所写的传统叙事 他说，我们应该走出疑古的时代。 但是关于我们是否可以信任司马 迁的争论是良性的。因为正是通过 这种批判性思考和质疑，我们才能 加深对历史的理解。 我认为，历史研究的目的不是为了 促进民族主义，也不是为了对过去 感觉良好。关键是要寻找真相，了 解人是什么样的，这样我们才能向

paragraphs.en: and understand what people are like, so that we can learn from them, and use that knowledge to make the world better in our lifetimes. And I hope I can encourage this kind of critical thinking in my own students. So before we close, let me just give you one more slide. I made a map with many of the major Chinese pre-historic sites on it. I hope this gives you a better sense of just how rich and how complex the prehistoric period in China is. And I hope it makes you want to learn more. Thank you all, very much! Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed this lesson on Chinese pre-history.
paragraphs.zh: 他们学习，并利用这些知识在我们 的有生之年让这个世界变得更美 好。 并且我希望我能鼓励我的学生也 具有这种批判性思维。 在我们结束之前，还有最后一张幻 灯片。 我做了一张地图，上面标记了许多 中国的史前遗址。 我希望这能让你更好地了解中国 史前时期的丰富和复杂程度。我希 望它能激发你的学习兴趣。 谢谢大家！ 好的，各位。希望你们喜欢这节关 于中国史前历史的课。现在我想与

paragraphs.en: Now I'd like to give you more resources. Perhaps the best museum of all for Chinese history is the National Museum in Beijing. I actually went to the National Museum two weeks ago. But I made the mistake of going on Saturday in the summer when the museum was really crowded. I think I will try to go back on a weekday morning, so that the crowds will be smaller and I will have more time to really think about the artifacts. If you go downstairs, the basement of the Museum is dedicated to Ancient Chinese history, and the first room you enter is all about the Neolithic and Pre-historic period. Many of the artifacts that I've shown
paragraphs.zh: 大家分享更多的资源。 关于中国历史，全世界最好的博物 馆应该就是北京的国家博物馆。 其实我两周前才去了国家博物馆， 但当时是暑期，又逢周末，博物馆 内人山人海。 我会在某个工作日的早晨再去一 次，这样就不会有那么多人，我可 以有更多的时间来仔细端详这些 文物。 博物馆的地下室是专门为中国古 代历史而建的，你进入的第一个展 厅就是关于新石器时代和史前时 期的。我在这张幻灯片中展示的很 多东西都可以在那里找到。

paragraphs.en: you in this slide show can be found there. For example, in the very first room, you can see this collection of pots in a display case along the wall. The pots become more recent as you go from left to right. On the far left is a pot from Xianren Cave. This may be the oldest pottery in China. Next is a pot from the Hemudu culture. Then a pot from Yangshao culture, along with a stone mortar. Then a pot from Da Wenkou culture. To the right of that is a pot from Longshan culture.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，在第一个展厅，你可以在墙 上的一个展示柜中看到这些陶罐 的集中展示。 从左往右，陶罐的年代与我们越来 越接近。 最左边的是仙人洞的陶罐，可能是 中国最古老的陶器。 接下来是来自河姆渡文化的陶罐。 然后是来自仰韶文化的陶罐，放在 一起的还有一个石臼。 它们旁边是来自大汶口文化的陶 罐。 大汶口陶罐的右侧是来自龙山文 化的陶罐。

paragraphs.en: And to the far right is a pot from Kesheng zhuang culture, which I actually did not even know existed, until I visited the museum. Go to the museum and check-out this display for yourself. But when you do, I want you to think about something. We humans have a habit of compressing the past in our minds. So if you look at these pots and don't stop to think, you might walk away thinking they are all from the same time period, since they are all in the same display case. But think about this: the pot from Xianren cave on the left was made around 12,000 BC. But the Longshan pot on the right
paragraphs.zh: 龙山陶罐的右侧是来自客省庄文 化的陶罐，在我参观博物馆之前， 我甚至都不知道这个文化遗址的 存在。 纸上得来终觉浅，绝知此事要躬 行。请自己去博物馆看看这个展览 吧！ 如果你真的去博物馆观察了，我希 望你能一边看，一边思考。 我们人类有在头脑中压缩过去的 习惯。 因此，如果你在观察时不停下来思 考，你可能会直接走过，并且认为 这些陶罐都来自同一时期——鉴 于它们都在同一个陈列柜中展览。 但是想想看：左边的仙人洞陶罐是 在公元前 12,000 年左右制造的。 但右侧的龙山陶罐是在公元前

paragraphs.en: was made around 2000 BC. So the pot on the left is 10,000 years older, than the pot on the right. By comparison, the Oracle bones from the Shang dynasty are only 3200 years old. So the Xianren pot and the Longshan pot are separated by 3 times as much time, as separates us from the Oracle Bones.
paragraphs.zh: 2000 年左右生产的，也就是说， 左侧的陶罐比它早了 1 万年。 相比之下，商朝的甲骨文只有 3200 年的历史。 因此，仙人洞陶罐与龙山陶罐之间 的时间距离，等于现代人与甲骨文 之间的三倍！

titleZh: 第十九讲：埃及走进“新”时代（新王朝时期）

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to return to Egypt and look at the next 1000 years of its history. Specifically, our focus will be on the 1st intermediate period, the Middle Kingdom, the 2nd Intermediate period and then the New Kingdom. We will cover a lot of history very fast, so all I will be able to do is summarize some of the main events for this part of Egyptian history. The last time we looked at Egypt, the Old Kingdom had just come to an end, and the country had slid into the Civil War and disunity of the 1st Intermediate Period.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。 今天我们将再度讨论古埃及，看看 接下来一千年的古埃及历史。 具体而言，我们将聚焦第一中间 期、中王朝时期、第二中间期，然 后是新王朝时期。 我们要很快地讲完很长一段历史， 所以我这部分对古埃及历史的讲 述将以概括重要历史事件为主。 上一次讲埃及，古王朝时期刚好结 束，国家陷入内战与分裂，这便是 第一中间期。

paragraphs.en: This period finally ended when Mentuhotep the II reunited the country and began the Middle Kingdom period of Egyptian history. Instead of building a pyramid, Mentuhotep II built a mortuary temple, as you can see in the picture on the right. After him there were a few more pyramids built, but the Egyptian Kings mostly built temples for their tombs instead. The great era of Pyramid building was largely over by the time of the Middle Kingdom. Instead the Kings of Egypt built temples and obelisks, and statues and other types of monuments. The Middle Kingdom of Egypt lasted for about 250 years.And
paragraphs.zh: 最终，门图霍特普二世终结了乱 世，统一了埃及，开启了中王朝时 期。 门图霍特普二世没有建造金字塔， 而是建造了祭祀神庙。如图所示。 在他之后人们又建起了几座金字 塔，然而古埃及的国王大部分都开 始建造庙宇作为坟墓了。 到了中王朝时期，建造金字塔的黄 金时代基本已经告终。 埃及的国王们转而建造庙宇、方尖 碑、雕像等其他类型纪念性遗迹。 埃及的中王朝时期持续了约 250 年，历任 10 位国王。

paragraphs.en: during that time there were 10 Kings. But after the 10th King, the Royal Family ran out of male heirs. So Sobekneferu, the sister of Amenemhat IV, the 10th King became the ruler instead. Sobekneferu was the first female monarch in Egyptian history. During the Middle Kingdom, the main focus of Egyptian foreign policy was their neighbor to the south, Nubia. The Nubians had darker skin than the Egyptians, and looked a little different as you can see from these two different statues. Their population was always lower than Egypt's because the land of the upper Nile is not as productive.
paragraphs.zh: 但是在第十任国王之后，皇室没有 男性继承人了。 于是，第十任国王阿蒙涅姆赫特四 世的妹妹塞贝克涅弗鲁继承了王 位。 塞贝克涅弗鲁是古埃及历史上首 位女性君主。 在中王朝时期，埃及的对外政策重 心主要放在其南部的邻国，努比 亚。 努比亚人比埃及人肤色更深，你可 以从这两尊雕像上看出二者的外 貌差异。 他们的人口总是少于埃及，因为尼 罗河上游的土壤的没有那么肥沃。

paragraphs.en: But they had gold, which the Egyptians wanted. Traditionally the southern boundary of Egypt was the first cataract. We talked about this in our first lesson on Egypt, and this was still the border when Mentuhotep II came to power. But the KingAmenemhat I sent his army into Nubia and pushed the boundary down to the 2nd cataract in the early 20th century BC. And his son, Senesret I pushed the boundary all the way down to the 3rd cataract. Unfortunately, when Sobekneferu died in 1802 BC, she died without an heir. And the Kingdom became divided once again.
paragraphs.zh: 但是他们拥有黄金，这是埃及人渴 求的东西。 人们传统上将第一大瀑布作为埃 及的南部边界。 我们在第一次讲埃及时提到过这 点。而当门图霍特普二世掌权时， 这里仍然是埃及的边界。 然而到了公元前 20 世纪初，国王 阿蒙涅姆赫特一世派遣军队入侵 努比亚，将边界向南推进到了第二 大瀑布。 而他的儿子，塞内斯莱特一世又一 直将埃及的版图扩张到了第三大 瀑布。 不幸的是，塞贝克涅弗鲁于公元前 1802 年去世，她身后亦无继承人。 帝国再度分崩离析。

paragraphs.en: The Middle Kingdom ended and the 2nd Intermediate period began. This period of disunity and civil war would last for 250 years until a strong king once more united Egypt and founded the New Kingdom. But during the disunity of the 2nd Intermediate period, Egypt suffered the first foreign invasion in its history. Agroup of people with horses, chariots and the compound bow invaded Egypt. These people were called the Hyksos, and they probably came from Canaan. However, before the 1600s BC, the Egyptians had never seen horses and their self-bows could not match the range of the Hyksos compound bow.
paragraphs.zh: 中王朝时期结束，第二中间期开 始。 这段分裂与内战的时期持续了 250 年，直到一位强大的君主再度 统一了埃及，建立了新王朝。 但是就在 第二中间期的分裂中， 古埃及还经历了史上首次异族侵 略。 一群人骑着马，驾着车，挽着复合 弓入侵了埃及。 这些人被称为希克索斯人，人们可 能来自迦南地区。 但是在公元前 16 世纪之前，古埃 及人还从未见过马，他们的单弓在 射程上也无法与希克索斯复合弓 相匹敌。

paragraphs.en: The Egyptians were overwhelmed by these powerful new weapons, and divided by civil war, they could not resist this foreign invasion. And then the Nubians saw their chance to take revenge for 200 years of bullying, and THEY attacked Egypt from the south. Divided by Civil War, intimidated by the horses, and attacked on two fronts, the Egyptians were crushed. By 1650BC, only the city of Thebes and its surrounding territory continued to be ruled by Native Egyptians. Northern Egypt was controlled by the Hyksos and Southern Egypt was ruled by the Nubians. This state of affairs lasted for 100 years until the great Egyptian King
paragraphs.zh: 埃及人被努比亚人的强劲武器全 面压制，加上内战导致国家分裂， 他们无力抵抗这次异族侵略。 这时，努比亚人已饱受埃及人的欺 凌达二百年，这次他们终于看到了 复仇的机会。于是他们也从南边攻 打了埃及。 埃及因内战而分裂，又受到战马的 威胁，还被两面夹击，终于被全面 击溃了。 到了公元前 1650 年，只有底比斯 及其周边区域仍然由埃及人统治。 埃及北部被希克索斯控制，而南部 被努比亚人占领。 这种内外交困的局面持续了一百 年，直到埃及出现了一位伟大的君

paragraphs.en: and military leader Ahmose I organized his army and attacked the Hyksos, winning a stunning victory against them, and driving them out of the Nile region. ThenAhmose I turned south and attacked the Nubians and drove them back again beyond the 3rd cataract. By the time he was doneAhmose I had reunified Egypt under native Egyptian rule and began the next period in Egyptian history, the New Kingdom. The New Kingdom period lasted from 1550 to 1077 BC, and it was during this time that the Egyptian Kingdom reached its greatest level of power. There were at least 30 Kings in the New Kingdom period; these are the
paragraphs.zh: 主和军事统帅——雅赫摩斯。他率 领他的军队抗击希克索斯人，取得 了完胜，将他们赶出了尼罗河流 域。 接着雅赫摩斯一世挥师南下，向努 比亚人发起进攻，将他们赶到了第 三大瀑布之外。 战争结束后，雅赫摩斯一世再度统 一了埃及，并翻开了古埃及史的新 篇章——新王朝时期。 新王朝时期从公元前 1550 年持续 到 1077 年。在这一时期，古埃及 王朝的实力达到了顶峰。 新王朝时期共有至少 30 位国王， 这是前 12 位。

paragraphs.en: first 12. And these are the next 18. Though the last King to have real power was Ramses III, after him the next 7 kings were ineffectual and their power became weaker and weaker. Maybe in a vain attempt to capture some of the power of their ancestors they all called themselves Ramses, but this did not save the Kingdom. And after 1077 BC, Egypt collapsed into the 3rd Intermediate period. Egypt did survive as an independent country for another 500 years, but it was never again a superpower. One of the most interesting rulers during the New Kingdom period was Hatshepsut.
paragraphs.zh: 这是接下来的 18 位。 然而最后一位掌握实权的国王是 拉美西斯三世，在他之后的 7 位国 王平庸无能，权力越来越弱。 也许是为了重拾先祖昔日的权力， 他们都做了徒劳的努力，都称自己 为拉美西斯。但这并没有拯救王朝 的命运。 到了公元前 1077 年，埃及进入了 第三中间期。 古埃及的确又作为独立的国家延 续了 500 年，但他再也没能成为一 个强大的帝国。 新王朝时期最有趣的一位统治者 是哈特谢普苏特。

paragraphs.en: When her husband died in 1478 BC she ruled Egypt directly for 20 years, which were focused on building and trade instead of war. Under her Egypt experienced a golden age of peace and prosperity. SinceAncient Egyptian had no word for "female king, Hatshepsut started using the word "Pharaoh" to describe her position. That word used to mean "The House of the King" but after her, it came to mean "The King of Egypt". Hatshepsut's successor and nephew, Thutmose III, was the opposite of his aunt. Thutmose loved war. He pushed the borders of Egypt all the way down to the 6th cataract, bringing most of Nubia under his control.
paragraphs.zh: 在公元前 1478 年，她的丈夫死后， 她开始了对埃及长达 20 年的直接 统治。这段时间埃及着重发展建筑 和贸易，而非战争。 在她的治理下，埃及经历了一段繁 荣与稳定的黄金时期。 由于古埃及没有“女王”这个词， 哈特谢普苏特开始使用“法老”这 个词来称呼自己的权位。 这个词的本意是“国王的房屋”， 然而在她之后，这个词的意思变成 了“埃及之王”。 哈特谢普苏特继承人是侄子图特 莫斯三世，他与姑姑的执政风格截 然相反。 图特莫斯是好战分子，他讲埃及的 版图一直扩张到了第六大瀑布，将 努比亚的大部分地区纳入了自己 的治下。

paragraphs.en: And he also pushed north and conquered Canaan, pushing Egypt's borders all the way to the Hittite Kingdom. Another interesting King was Akhenhaten. He decided that traditional Egyptian religion was wrong, and that there was only 1 God,Aten, the god of the sun. He built a new capital city and tried to get all of his countrymen to change their religious beliefs. He also initiated an experimental phase in Egyptian art. He commissioned statues of himself holding hands with his wife Nefertiti, who he deeply loved. And carvings in stone took on a strange, curvy, elongated style.
paragraphs.zh: 并且他也向北进行扩张，占领了迦 南地区，将埃及的北部边境推进到 了赫梯王国。 还有一位有趣的国王是阿克何纳 托。 他裁定古埃及传统的宗教是错误 的。世上唯一的真神是太阳神阿托 恩。 他建造 了一座新的首都，以使他 所有的子民改变宗教信仰。 他还在埃及开启了艺术领域的新 尝试。 他下令凿刻他与爱妻娜芙蒂蒂手 腕手的雕像。 石刻的风格奇异，线条风格扭曲细 长。

paragraphs.en: He also seemed to want images of him to be realistic, and so many statues of him depict his bulging belly, instead of an ideal body. But his reign didn't last. AfterAkhenhaten died, his son Tutankhamun became King. Tutankhamun was only 10 years old when he assumed the throne, and that made it easy for the priests to manipulate him. He overturned all of his father's religious reforms and Egyptians went back to worshipping the old gods. Tutankhamun's tomb was discovered in 1922 by the archaeologist Howard Carter. Tutankhamun is one of the most famous Egyptian kings because his
paragraphs.zh: 此外他似乎也想让自己的形象写 实一点，很多他的雕像都有隆起的 肚腩，而不是理想的身材。 然而他的统治并不长久。 阿克何纳托死后，他的儿子图坦卡 门继承了王位。 图坦卡门即位时年仅 10 岁，这让 他容易受到祭司们的控制。 他推翻了父亲进行的所有宗教改 革，埃及重新崇拜起了旧神。 图坦卡门的墓葬于 1922 年被霍华 德·卡特发现。 图坦卡门是最负盛名的埃及国王 之一，因为他的墓葬被卡特开启时

paragraphs.en: tomb was undisturbed when Carter opened it. This meant that it was full of amazing treasures and artifacts, all more than 3000 years old. Here is a picture of the tomb as Carter first saw it. And here are some of the amazing artifacts he brought up. Tutankhamen's body, his golden bed, his golden death mask, jewelry, and a beautiful chest. These treasures are now all in museums in Egypt. And the greatest of all the New Kingdom Kings was Ramses II. He fought a war with the Hittites over control of Canaan. The battle that they fought in 1274
paragraphs.zh: 仍旧完好无损。 这意味着里面堆满了令人惊叹的 宝藏，它们都有 3000 多年的历史。 这是一张卡特初次发现墓葬时照 片。 而这是一些他发掘出的令人惊异 的工艺品。 其中包括图坦卡门的尸身、他的黄 金床、黄金面具、珠宝，还有一口 漂亮的箱子。 这些珍宝现在都藏于埃及的博物 馆中。 新王朝时期最伟大的国王是拉美 西斯二世。 为了争夺迦南地区，他与赫梯人打 了一仗。 战斗发生于公元前 1274 年，被称

paragraphs.en: BC, the Battle of Kadesh, was both the largest chariot battle in history, AND the first battle for which we have detailed knowledge of the tactics and the order of events. 16 years later, Ramses also signed a peace treaty with the Hittites, which brought this war to a close. Fortunately, we have both the Egyptian and the Hittite copy of this peace treaty, which is the oldest peace treaty in history. Acopy of this treaty is actually on display at the United Nations headquarters in New York City. So by the time of Ramses II, the Eastern Mediterranean has reached a high level of development. There were four great powers: The Egyptians, theAssyrians, the Hittites
paragraphs.zh: 为卡迭石战役。这既是史上规模最 大的一场车战，同时也是第一场有 详细记载了战术和事件先后顺序 的战役。 16 年之后，拉美西斯与赫梯人签 署了和平条约，终结了这张战争。 幸运的是，这份和平条约双方的版 本都流传下来，这也是史上最古老 的和平条约。 在纽约市的联合国总部中，就展出 着这份条约的仿制品。 在拉美西斯二世时期，地中海东部 的发展水平已经很高了。 该地区有四大势力：古埃及、亚述、 赫梯，和迈锡尼。还有数不清的小

paragraphs.en: and the Mycenaeans.And they were numerous smaller powers. There were four great powers: The Egyptians, theAssyrians, the Hittites and the Mycenaeans.And they were numerous smaller powers. These Kingdoms conducted diplomacy with each other, made alliances and peace treaties, conducted long-distance trade, and together formed the first international system in world history. But then, around 1200 BC, this whole system came tumbling down, and the Bronze Age World suffered a devastating collapse. The exact cause is still something of a mystery, but please join me next time as we try to understand what happened, and come to terms with
paragraphs.zh: 势力。 该地区有四大势力：古埃及、亚述、 赫梯，和迈锡尼。还有数不清的小 势力。 这些王国互相外教、结盟、缔结和 约、长途贸易，共同形成了世界历 史上的首个国际体系。 但在当时，公元前 1200 年前后， 这个体系土崩瓦解，并且青铜时代 的世界也经历了毁灭性的崩溃。 具体的原因仍然是个谜，但是请继 续收听我的下一讲，我们来试图理 解一下这段历史，浅析这场终结了 青铜时代，并将地中海地区推向黑

paragraphs.en: the catastrophe which brought the BronzeAge to a close and ushered the Mediterranean world into a Dark Age. So join me next time for the last lesson in this series: The Bronze Age Collapse. I hope you enjoyed this review of Egyptian history. Because ancient Egyptian artifacts are so highly prized, many of the artifacts I showed you in this lesson are scattered around the world in great museums. Of course this includes the Louvre and the British Museum, but it also includes some that we haven't talked about so far, so let me show you some of those. The statues ofAhmose I and
paragraphs.zh: 暗时代的大灾难。 敬请期待该系列课的最后一讲：青 铜时代的崩溃。 希望你喜欢这期古埃及历史串讲。 由于古埃及的艺术品极具价值，我 这一讲中展示的很多艺术品都分 散在世界各大博物馆中。 当然，这其中包含卢浮宫和大英博 物馆，但是也有一些我未曾提及的 地方，我现在要介绍给大家。 雅赫摩斯一世和哈特谢普苏特的

paragraphs.en: Hatshepsut are kept in the Metropolitan Museum ofArt in New York City. This statue ofAmenhotep can be found in the Museum of FineArts in Boston. The Luxor Museum in Egypt contains many treasures, including this statue of Thutmose III. The Brooklyn Museum in New York City is another world-class museum, and it also has this statue of Amenhotep II. And the Museum ofArt History in Vienna,Austria holds this statue of Horemheb. Okay, that concludes our lesson, thank you all very much!
paragraphs.zh: 雕像现藏于纽约的大都会艺术馆。 这尊阿蒙霍特普的雕像可以在波 士顿美术馆找到。 埃及的卢克索博物馆也收藏着许 多艺术珍品，包括这尊图特摩斯三 世的雕像。 纽约的布鲁克林博物馆也是一家 世界级的博物馆，这里也收藏着阿 蒙霍特普二世的雕像。 奥地利维也纳的艺术历史博物馆 珍藏这这尊霍伦赫布像。 好了，这一讲到此为止，谢谢大家！

titleZh: 第二十讲：青铜时代的凋零

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, welcome back to our series on World History. Today is the 20th and final lecture in our series on the BronzeAge. We will mainly be discussing the years between 1250 and 1050 BC. Here is what that looks like on our big timeline. By the 1300s BC, the Hittite Empire was strong and powerful. The New Kingdom of Egypt was in full swing. The Mycenaean Greeks were at the peak of their power, and the Assyrians were the dominant power in Mesopotamia. All of these Kingdoms traded with each other and conducted diplomacy. Together they formed the first International System in human
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天是我们关于青铜时代《古 代文明史》系列讲座的第 20 讲， 也是最后一节课。 我 们 将 主 要 讨 论 公 元 前 1250-1050 年之间的历史。这是我 们的大时间表上的样子。 到公元前 14 世纪，赫梯帝国强大 有力。埃及的新王国正如火如荼地 发展着，迈锡尼希腊人处于鼎盛时 期，而亚述人是美索不达米亚的主 导力量。 所有这些王国相互贸易，进行外 交。他们共同组成了人类历史上第 一个国际体系。

paragraphs.en: history. We know they traded with each other because we find Egyptian artifacts in Greece, Greek artifacts in Canaan, Hittite artifacts in Cyprus, and so on. They also wrote letters to each other discussing their trade. But the most powerful evidence of all for this trade is probably the Uluburun shipwreck. This is a picture of the Uluburun shipwreck. It was a merchant ship that sank sometime around 1300 BC. It was carrying goods around the Mediterranean as part of this First International System and trade network. But for some reason it sank with all of its goods still onboard, and came
paragraphs.zh: 我们知道他们互相交易，因为我们 在希腊发现了埃及的文物，在迦南 发现了希腊文物，在塞浦路斯发现 了赫梯文物，等等。 他们还互相通信讨论贸易事宜。 但其中最有力的证据，还要数乌鲁 布伦沉船。 这是乌鲁布伦沉船的照片，这是一 艘商船，于公元前 1300 年左右沉 没。作为首个国际体系和贸易网络 的一部分，它在地中海周围运送货 物。 但出于某种原因，它和船上所有的 货物，永远地长眠于 60 米深的海

paragraphs.en: to rest 60 meters under the water. The Uluburun shipwreck is the oldest shipwreck ever discovered by archaeology, and it is such an important discovery that they actually built a replica of the ship and sank it in order to better understand the wreckage. I'm including a picture of that replica because I think it gives you a better idea of what the ship looked like in the BronzeAge itself. Here is a picture of an underwater archaeologist excavating the site. The ship was studied and the artifacts retrieved during a project that lasted from 1984 to 1994, and required over 20,000 dives to complete. The ship was carrying a rich collection of goods. It was carrying
paragraphs.zh: 底。 乌鲁布伦沉船是考古学史上发现 过最古老的沉船。它如此珍贵，以 至于学者们重建了一艘一模一样 的船，并将其沉入海底，以便更准 确地研究。 这是一张复制品的照片，因为我认 为它能让你们更好地了解青铜时 代的船是什么样子的。 这是一幅水下考古学家在挖掘现 场的照片。在从 1984-1994 年的一 个项目中，通过 2 万多次的潜水， 这些文物被重新取回。 这艘船装载了大量的货物。其中有 10 吨铜锭和 1 吨锡锭，这是制造

paragraphs.en: 10 tons of copper ingots, and 1 ton of tin ingots, which is the proper proportion for making bronze. But it was also carrying glass ingots, Egyptian gold, terebinth resin, Syrian ivory, Cypriot and Canaanite pottery. All in all, the ship was carrying trade goods from 7 different cultures. This is conclusive proof that extensive trade networks connected the entire eastern Mediterranean into a broader economic system. And this trade was vital to the proper functioning of these societies, because the main material of the age -Bronze- is made from copper and tin. But both of these metals are rare, tin especially so. Most of the region's copper came from the
paragraphs.zh: 青铜的合适比例。 同时它还载有玻璃铸锭，埃及的黄 金，松节油树脂，叙利亚象牙，塞 浦路斯和迦南的陶器。 总而言之，这艘船运载着来自 7 种不同文化的贸易货物。 这是一个确凿的证据，证明广泛的 贸易网络将整个东地中海连接成 一个更广大的经济体系。 这种贸易对这些社会的正常运转 是至关重要的，因为这个时代的主 要材料——青铜——是用铜和锡 制成的。但这两种金属都很稀有， 尤其是锡。该地区的大部分铜都来 自塞浦路斯，而锡是从遥远的阿富 汗进口的。

paragraphs.en: Island of Cyprus, and tin was imported from as far away as Afghanistan. Copper and Tin were as important to their economies as oil is to us. But then this whole system was disrupted beginning around the year 1200 BC. Recent research has shown that Europe and the Middle East experienced a dry period that began in the late 1200s BC and lasted for almost 300 years. The drought led to less rain, this led to crop failure, which led to famine. We have evidence of this problem in some of the letters that we have unearthed from this time period. The King of the Hittites wrote this message:
paragraphs.zh: 铜和锡对他们经济的重要程度，就 和石油对于我们一样。 但是整个系统在公元前 1200 年左 右就被打乱了。 最近的研究表明，欧洲和中东经历 了一段始于公元前 13 世纪晚期的 干旱，持续了近 300 年。 干旱使得降雨减少，这导致了作物 歉收，最终引发了饥荒。 我们从这个时期发现了一些信件， 其中有证据证明了这场干旱的存 在。 赫梯国王写了这样一句话：“你不 知道我的土地上有饥荒吗？”

paragraphs.en: "Do you not know that there was a famine in the midst of my lands?”" The King of Ugarit wrote this: “Here with me, plenty has become famine.” And inland from Ugarit, in the town of Emar, an agent for a prominent Merchant in Ugarit wrote a desperate letter: "There is a famine in our house; we will all die of hunger. If you do not quickly arrive here, we ourselves will die of hunger.You will not see a living soul from your land.” On the Island of Cyprus, the drought became so bad that large parts of the island were uninhabitable and were abandoned. This disrupted the supply of copper, since Cyprus was the region's main producer.
paragraphs.zh: 乌加里特国王写道：“在我的国度， 我的子民正和我一同遭遇饥荒。” 在乌加里特的一个内陆城镇—— 埃马尔，一个著名的代理商写下了 这样一封绝望的信： “我的家庭正在遭受饥荒，我们都 将死于饥饿。如果你不尽快赶到， 我们所有人都会饿死。在你的土地 上，你将看不到一个活着的灵魂。” 在塞浦路斯岛上，旱灾非常严重， 以致于岛上的大部分地区无法居 住。这扰乱了铜的供应，因为塞浦 路斯是该地区铜的主要生产国。

paragraphs.en: And then the famine led some people to pack up their possessions and begin migrating in search of food. One important historical pattern is that hungry people can bring down a society, because they are desperate and have nothing left to lose. These migrating people had a big effect on the Mediterranean world. The best evidence of this comes from the tomb of Ramses III at a place called Medinet Habu, close to the modern city of Luxor. Here is what Medinat Habu looks like today. On one side of this tomb, Ramses had a giant inscription carved into the walls. Its hard to see in a photograph, so
paragraphs.zh: 然后，饥荒导致一些人收拾好他们 的财产，开始迁徙，寻找食物。 一个重要的历史模式是，饥饿的人 可以拖垮一个社会，因为他们绝望 了，没有什么可失去的。 这些移民对地中海地区产生了巨 大的影响。最有力的证据来自于拉 美西斯三世葬祭殿，靠近现代城市 卢克索。 这是拉美西斯三世葬祭殿今天的 样子。 在这个墓穴的一边，拉美西斯在墙 上刻了一个巨大的碑文。 从照片中很难看清楚文字，这是一

paragraphs.en: here is a drawing of what that inscription looks like. As you can see here, it shows Ramses III, who is drawn much bigger than everyone else, shooting a bow and arrow at an invading army of people in boats. These people who he is fighting are called "The Sea People". On the inscription, Ramses III writes: The foreign countries (i.e. Sea Peoples) made a conspiracy in their islands. All at once the lands were removed and scattered in the fray. No land could stand before their arms: from Khatte, Qode, Carchemish, Arzawa and Alashiya on, being cut off (i.e. destroyed) at one time. A camp was set up in Amurru. They desolated its people,
paragraphs.zh: 幅画，展示了碑文的内容。 如你所见，画中的拉美西斯三世比 其他人都要大得多，他正在向一群 船上的入侵者射箭，这些人被称为 “海上民族”。 碑文中，拉美西斯三世写道： 异国人（海上民族）在他们的岛上 秘密制造了阴谋。一瞬间，土地四 分五裂。猛烈的攻势下，没有任何 地区能够独善其身：从卡特、寇德、 卡尔凯美什、阿扎瓦，到阿莱西亚， 都一下子被切断（即被摧毁）。他 们在阿穆路建立了大本营，当地的 人民遭受驱赶，那片土地就如从未 存在过一般。他们向埃及进发，火 焰四起，先人而行。他们的同盟有

paragraphs.en: and its land was like that which has never come into being. They were coming forward toward Egypt, while the flame was prepared before them. Their confederation was the Peleset, Tjekker, Shekelesh, Denyen and Weshesh, lands united. They laid their hands upon the land as far as the circuit of the earth, their hearts confident and trusting: 'Our plans will succeed! Now we have some idea about where these places that Ramses mentions might be. We are most certain that the Peleset refers to the Philistines, who come originally from northern Greece. Weshesh, might refer to the ancient name of Troy, Wilusa. Perhaps these were refugees fleeing the Trojan war.
paragraphs.zh: 派莱赛特人、阐卡尔人、舍克利斯 人、达奴人和万舍斯人，他们的土 地紧紧联盟。他们将手放在土地 上，心中充满自信与信任：“我们 的计划会成功的！” 现在我们对拉美西斯提到的这些 地方有了一定的了解。 如今我们最能确定的是，“派莱赛 特人”指的是非利士人，来自希腊 北部。 万舍斯人，可能是对特洛伊和威卢 萨的旧称。他们可能是特洛伊战争 的难民。

paragraphs.en: Denyen, might be the Danaan Greeks. Tjekker might be southern Italy. Shardana might be the Island of Sardinia. And Shekelesh might be the island of Sicily. In any case, we think that all these people came together and formed a confederation of "Sea Peoples" Their homelands were probably experiencing famine because of the drought.And so the people became migrants, looking for a better place to live. But of course, nobody wants to suddenly welcome hundreds of thousands of refugees into their own country, so the Sea People came together as an army that was willing
paragraphs.zh: 达奴人，可能是指迦南希腊人。 阐卡尔人可能来自意大利南部。 莎达娜可能是指撒丁岛。 而舍克利斯可能是指西西里岛。 总之，我们认为这些人共同组成了 “海上民族”联盟。 由于干旱，他们的祖国可能正在经 历饥荒。于是人们离开故乡，想找 个更好的地方居住。 但是，当然，没有人愿意突然欢迎 成千上万的难民进入祖国，所以海 民们聚集在一起，作为一支愿意为 新家园而战的军队。

paragraphs.en: to fight for a new homeland. And this army travelled across the Eastern Mediterranean, disrupting trade, attacking cities, settling down in some places, and pillaging others. The Sea peoples came twice in Egyptian history. Once in 1207 BC and again 30 years later in 1177 BC. The Egyptians were strong enough to fight them off both times. The second time, it was Ramses III who fought them off, and this is what he says. "I overthrew those who invaded from their lands. I slew the Denyen (who are) in their isles, the Tjekker and the Peleset were made ashes. The Shardana and the Weshesh of the sea; they were made as those that exist not, taken captive at one time, brought as captives to Egypt, like
paragraphs.zh: 这支军队穿越东地中海，扰乱贸 易，袭击城市，在一些地方定居下 来，掠夺了其他地方。 在埃及历史上，海民出现过两次。 第一次是公元前 1207 年，第二次 是 30 年后的公元前 1177 年。两次 袭击都被埃及打回去了。 海民第二次入侵时，是拉美西斯三 世将其击退。 我推翻了侵略者。我在他们的岛上 杀死了达奴人，将阐卡尔人和派莱 赛特人烧为灰烬。莎达娜和万舍斯 人，他们像海边的沙石一样，被掳 到埃及。我将他们安置到我的据点 中，将他们分成成千上万个级别。 我每年都要向他们所有人征税，从 商店、粮仓、衣服和谷物中征税。

paragraphs.en: the sand of the shore. I settled them in strongholds bound in my name. Numerous were their classes like hundred-thousands. I taxed them all, in clothing and grain from the store-houses and granaries each year." This battle, where the Egyptians defeated the army of the Sea People is known as the Battle of Djahy, and it happened around the year 1177 BC. The Egyptians were able to successfully defend their homeland of the Nile against these invaders. But the Egyptians were also the rulers of Canaan, and they were unable to protect these more distant parts of their empire. In fact, we have evidence that Canaan was ravaged by the Sea
paragraphs.zh: 在这场战役中，埃及人击败了海民 军队。这场战役被称为“达贾伊之 战”，发生于公元前 1177 年。 埃及人成功地保卫了尼罗河的家 园，抵御这些入侵者。但是埃及人 也是迦南的统治者，他们无法保护 边陲地带。 事实上，从乌加里特国王的一封信 中，我们发现了迦南被海民蹂躏的

paragraphs.en: Peoples in a letter from the King of Ugarit. Ugarit was a major trading city, and a vassal of Egypt. But when the Sea Peoples came, the Egyptians were unable to send help.And so the King of Ugarit wrote a desperate letter to the King of Cyprus asking for help. The letter was never sent, because just hours after it was written, the palace and city of Ugarit caught fire. The burning city baked the letter and then it tumbled into the basement and lay there for 3200 years until archaeologists dug it up in the 20th century. My father, behold, the enemy's ships came (here); my cities(?) were burned, and they did evil things in my country. Does not my father know that all my troops and
paragraphs.zh: 证据。 乌加特是一个主要的贸易城市，是 埃及的附属国。但当海民入侵时， 埃及人却无法提供帮助。于是，乌 加里特国王在绝望中给塞浦路斯 国王写信请求帮助。 然而，这封信从未寄出。仅在写成 后数个小时内，宫殿和城市就着火 了。燃烧的城市焚毁了这封信，它 在地下静卧了 3200 年，直到 20 世纪被考古学家挖了出来。 我的父亲，看呀，敌人的船只来到 了（这里），我的城市被焚毁，他 们在我的国土犯下滔天大罪。难道 我的父亲不知道吗？我的战车都 在哈提，我的船只都在卢卡。因此，

paragraphs.en: chariots(?) are in the Land of Hatti, and all my ships are in the Land of Lukka ... Thus, the country is abandoned to itself. May my father know it: the seven ships of the enemy that came here inflicted much damage upon us. He calls the king of Cyprus "father" because that was how a weaker King addressed a more powerful king in the late Bronze Age, and the King of Ugarit had been abandoned by the Egyptians and was desperate for any help he could get. But it wouldn't have done much good even if the letter had reached Cyprus, the Cypriots were also in a terrible situation and their own cities were experiencing the same sort of destruction. As you can see from this map, in the
paragraphs.zh: 这个国家被抛弃了。愿我父知晓： 敌人的七艘战船，给我们带来了无 穷伤害。 他称塞浦路斯国王为“父亲”，因 为在青铜时代晚期，实力较弱的国 王就是这样称呼一位更强大的国 王的，而乌加里特国王已经被埃及 人抛弃，迫切希望得到任何可能的 帮助。 但是，即使这封信到达了塞浦路 斯，情况也不会有太大的好转。塞 浦路斯的情况也很糟糕，他们自己 的城市也经历了同样的破坏。 从这张地图上可以看出，在青铜时

paragraphs.en: late Bronze age, the Fertile Crescent was one of the most urbanized and developed places in the world. It was thickly settled with cities and towns. But then, in the terrible hundred years between 1250 and 1150 BC, almost every city in this region was burned to the ground. This was a wave of destruction that had been unparalleled in history so far. And again, the Medinat Habu inscription gives us some clues about what happened. Ramses III writes: No land could stand before their arms: from Khatte, Qode, Carchemish, Arzawa and Alashiya on, being cut off (i.e. destroyed) at one time. A camp was set up in Amurru. They desolated its people,
paragraphs.zh: 代晚期，新月沃土是世界上城市化 程度最高、最发达的地方之一。这 里密集地分布着城市和村镇。 但是，在公元前 1250-1150 年这可 怕的一百年间，这个地区几乎所有 的城市都被夷为平地。这是一场前 所未有的破坏浪潮。 再一次，拉美西斯三世葬祭殿的石 碑给我们提供了一些线索。 拉美西斯三世这样写道： 在猛烈的攻势下，没有任何地区能 够独善其身：从卡特、寇德、卡尔 凯美什、阿扎瓦，到阿莱西亚，都 一下子被切断（即被摧毁）。他们 在阿穆路建立了大本营，当地的人 民遭受驱赶，那片土地就如从未存

paragraphs.en: and its land was like that which has never come into being. These places are names for regions north of Egypt. Arazawa is the western coast of Anatolia. Khatte is the interior of Anatolia, it refers to the Hittite Kingdom.Alishiya is the island of Cyprus. Amuru and Qode refer to what is now the border region between Turkey and Syria, and Carchemish is northern Syria. Ramses III says that these regions were "cut off" and their people were "desolated" and it became like a land which never existed. All of this fits with the archaeological evidence for all those cities being destroyed in these exact places.
paragraphs.zh: 在过一般。 这些地方是埃及北部地区的名字。 阿拉扎瓦是安纳托利亚的西部海 岸；卡特位于安纳托利亚内部，指 赫梯王国。阿莱西亚是塞浦路斯 岛，阿穆路和寇德是土耳其和叙利 亚之间的边境地区，卡尔凯美什位 于叙利亚北部。 拉姆西斯三世说，这些地区被“切 断”，这里的人民被“驱赶”，变成 了一个从未存在过的土地。 这些描述都与考古证据相吻合，证 据表明，在文中提到的这些位置， 城市遭到了摧毁。

paragraphs.en: So maybe the Sea People first attacked Canaan in the 1190s and 1180s BC and they pillaged and destroyed that region. And after they had destroyed and pillaged everything that they could, they turned south and attacked Egypt's heartland, where they were finally stopped in 1177BC at the Battle of Djahy. But this time period is so terrible because it was not just Canaan that suffered this wave of destruction. Mycenaean Greece also was ravaged during this same time period. Troy was the first to burn, as we know from the archaeological evidence and from The Illiad. But a generation later, most of the great cities of Greece were also destroyed.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，也许海上民族在公元前 12 世纪 90-80 年代袭击了迦南，洗劫 并摧毁了这个地区。 在他们摧毁并尽其所能地掠夺一 切之后，他们转向南方，袭击了埃 及的中心地带。最后，他们终于在 公元前 177 年的达贾伊战役中被 阻止了。 这段时间非常可怕，因为不仅是迦 南遭到了洗劫，连迈锡尼希腊也在 同一时间遭到了破坏。 我们从《伊利亚特》中得知，特洛 伊是第一个被焚毁的。但一代人之 后，希腊的大部分城市都被摧毁 了。

paragraphs.en: Gla, Orchomenos, Thebes, Tiryns, Pylos, Knossos and Mycenae itself were all attacked during this time period, and all of these cities were burned to the ground. As far as I can tell, onlyAthens escaped destruction during this time. You see, the Greeks were also being invaded by barbarians who came from the north. Because they spoke a dialect of Greek that we now call "Doric Greek", we call these people the "Dorians". Perhaps the Dorians were the people who burned all the Mycenaean cities, or perhaps they just took advantage of the destruction and moved in. They may also be a part of the "Sea
paragraphs.zh: 在这段时间里，格拉、奥尔霍迈诺 斯、底比斯、梯林斯、皮洛斯、克 诺索斯和迈锡尼都遭受攻击，所有 这些城市都被夷为平地。 据我所知，只有雅典在这段时间里 逃脱了毁灭。 你看，希腊人也被来自北方的野蛮 人侵略了。 由于他们所说的希腊方言现在被 称为“多利安式希腊语”，我们将 这些人称为多利安人。 也许是多利安人烧毁了迈锡尼城 市，也许他们只是在城市被破坏后 搬了进去。 他们也可能是“海洋民族”的一部

paragraphs.en: Peoples", but it might also be that the Dorians, like the Sea Peoples, were just part of a larger mass-migration which was disrupting the entire world at that point. Mycenaean civilization collapsed; the great palaces burned; there were no more kings; trade collapsed; and the population shrank. Cities were abandoned and people went back to living in tribes and villages. And most importantly of all, the Greeks forgot the knowledge of writing. Linear B disappeared, and Greece entered a dark age without any written records. The Hittites too were overcome by this wave of destruction.
paragraphs.zh: 分，但也有可能像海洋民族一样， 多利安人只是更大规模移民的一 部分，在当时扰乱了整个世界。 迈锡尼文明陨落了。豪华的宫殿烧 成了灰烬，国王不复存在，贸易崩 溃，人口也萎缩了。 城市被遗弃，人们回到部落和村庄 生活。 最重要的是，希腊人失去了文字。 B 类线形文字消失了，希腊进入了 一个没有任何书面记录的黑暗时 代。 赫梯人也没能挺过这波破坏浪潮。

paragraphs.en: Their region was beset by famine, and their trading partners were destroyed by the Sea Peoples. Weakened as they were, the Hittites were defeated again and again by Assyrians, and their territory began to slip away. And then, another group of people called the Phrygians attacked them from the north. In 1178 BC, The Phrygians attacked the capital city of Hattusa itself. They overwhelmed the garrison and killed the last Hittite King, Suppiluliuma III. Then they pillaged the city and burned it to the ground. And that was the end of the Hittites. Now let's compare.
paragraphs.zh: 他们的地区被饥荒所困扰，他们的 贸易伙伴被海洋民族摧毁。 因此，赫梯人一次次地被亚述人打 败，他们的领土也随之越来越小。 随后，另一个民族——弗里吉亚人 从北方入侵了。 公元前 1178 年，弗里吉亚人袭击 了首都哈图萨。他们击垮了卫戍部 队，杀死了最后一任赫梯国王：苏 庇路里乌玛三世。 然后，他们洗劫了这座城市，并把 它夷为平地。 这就是赫梯人的末日。 现在让我们来比较一下。

paragraphs.en: This what the late BronzeAge world looked like in 1300 BC. As you can see, we have many different kingdoms, some great and some small. But they are all connected in a flourishing system of trade, and diplomacy. But then, after the Bronze Age Collapse in 1100 BC, only Egypt andAssyria are left. And Egypt has lost control of its empire, and now only rules its heartland of the Nile River valley. This weakening was so dramatic, that its marks the end of the New Kingdom of Egypt. Egypt will rule itself for another 500 years, but it will never again be a super-power. All across the Mediterranean world,
paragraphs.zh: 这就是公元前 1300 年的青铜时代 晚期。如你所见，当时有许多王国， 或大或小，都是在一个繁荣的贸易 体系和外交体系中联系起来的。 公元前 1100 年，青铜时代崩塌之 后，只有埃及和亚述得以幸存。 埃及已经失去了对其帝国的控制， 现在只统治着尼罗河流域的中心 地带。 这种弱化充满了戏剧性，标志着埃 及新王国的终结。 埃及还将继续自治 500 年，但它已 不再是一个超级大国。 在整个地中海世界，文明之光熄灭

paragraphs.en: the light of civilization went out. And a DarkAge began that would last for 400 years. But the Dark Ages would end eventually. And when civilization recovered around 800 BC, the BronzeAge was over. People were now using iron instead. New ideas like Monotheism, the Alphabet, and Democracy were being born. The Classical Period had begun. But THAT is a story for another time. One I will tell you next semester. Thank you very much for listening. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson on the Bronze Age Collapse. If you want to learn more,
paragraphs.zh: 了。一个黑暗的时代开始了，并且 持续了 400 年。 但黑暗时代终将结束。 当文明于公元前 800 年左右复兴 时，青铜时代就结束了。 人们现在用的是铁。一神论、字母 系统和民主这样的新思想诞生了。 古典时期开始了。 但那将是另一个时代的故事。我会 在下一期系列课程中与你讲述。 谢谢大家！ 好了，同学们，希望你们喜欢请青 铜时代的崩塌这一课。如果你想了 解更多，我有一些材料可以推荐给

paragraphs.en: I have some materials to recommend. My first recommendation is probably the best recent book on the topic. 1177 BC: The Year Civilization Collapsed. It was written by the archaeologist and historian Eric Cline in 2014. The author, Eric Cline also gave an hour-long lecture on the topic in 2016, which you can see for yourself on Youtube. I also recommend the series on the BronzeAge Collapse by the Youtube channel Extra Credit. Finally, Crash Course world history has a good video on the topic as well.
paragraphs.zh: 你。 我的第一个推荐是一本关于这一 主题的近代书。 《公元前 1177 年：文明崩溃了》。 它是由考古学家、历史学家埃里 克·克莱因于 2014 年写成的。 作者还在 2016 年就此主题作了一 场 1 小时的演讲，你可以在油管上 看到这个视频。 我还推荐油管频道 Extra Credit 上关于青铜时代崩塌的系列节目。 history 也推出了不错的视频。

paragraphs.en: There are also three research papers which have come out in the last 6 years, they provide strong support for the idea that a terrible drought was the main cause for the Bronze Age Collapse. If you want to explore the hard science behind this story, these are good place to start. Okay, those are all of my recommendations. Thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 在过去的 6 年里，也有三篇研究论 文发表，它们有力地支持了一种观 点，即严重的干旱是导致青铜时代 崩塌的主要原因。 如果你想要探索这个故事背后的 硬科学，这些都是很好的开始。 好了，这些都是我的建议。谢谢大 家！

titleZh: 第十四讲：带着黄金面具的迈锡尼文明

paragraphs.en: Hello, and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today we are going to be talking about the people who conquered the Minoans, the Mycenaean Greeks. But we won't just talk about them, we will also look at some of the other people who lived near them around the Aegean Sea: The Hittites and the Trojans.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。今天我们要讲的，是在 上一节课中征服了米诺斯文明的 民族，迈锡尼希腊人。但我们的目 光将不限于此：我们之后还要去看 看同样生活在爱琴海周边地区的 赫梯人和特洛伊人。

paragraphs.en: On our timeline, we will be looking at 800 years of history, from about 2000 BC until 1200 BC, which also marks the end of the Bronze Age, and the beginning of a Dark age in Western History. Here is what that looks like on a timeline: The story of the Greeks begins around 2000 BC. A tribe of people who spoke an Indo-European language move south into Greece from the Balkans.
paragraphs.zh: 我们将回顾时间线上公元前 2000-1200年之间这段800年的历 史，在此期间，青铜时代告一段落， 西方历史上的一个黑暗时代开始 了。 我们来看看时间线： 公元前 2000 年左右，一个说印欧 语系的部落从巴尔干半岛向南进 入了希腊，古希腊人的故事由此开 始。
